The sea between the mound and Maeshima is known as Karakoto Seto or Ushimado Seto.}]]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300| height =250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | label1=Ushimado Tenjinyama | label-pos1=bottom | label-color1=black | mark1=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.svg | mark-size1=25 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | label2=Kuroshima | label-pos2=top | label-color2=black | mark2=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.svg | mark-size2=25 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | label3=Kahoyama | label-pos3=bottom | label-color3=black | mark3=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.svg | mark-size3=25 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | label4=(Hakayama) | label-pos4=bottom | label-color4=black | mark4=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.svg | mark-size4=25 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | label5=Futazukayama | label-pos5=right | label-color5=black | mark5=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.svg | mark-size5=25 }} '''Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun''' (牛窓天神山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓}}. It is designated as a Setouchi City Historic Site under the name "Tenjinyama Kofun." == Overview == This Kofun is located on the ridge of a hill at the eastern edge of Ushimado Bay, southeast Okayama Prefecture.[http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/kurashi/kankorekishi/rekishi/jyuyobunkazai/shisekikoukou/shijyuyo/1418099490778.html Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun] (Setouchi City website). Ushimado Bay contains five keyhole-shaped Kofuns: this mound, {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}, {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳}}. Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun is the oldest among them.On-site explanation board. The Ushimado Tenjin Shrine is located on its southern side. No formal excavations have been conducted. The mound has a primitive keyhole type, with its front section undeveloped and flat, oriented south toward the sea. The rear section is believed to have two tiers, while the front section has one. Surface features include stone cover (rounded cobblestones) and haniwa fragments (cylindrical and jar-shaped). The burial facility is estimated to be a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, with slab-like stones visible at the rear section's summit. Artifacts have not been clearly identified. The construction is estimated to date to the mid-to-late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the early Kofun period. Its strategic placement overlooking the sea passage between Ushimado Bay and Maeshima, known as "Karakoto Seto" or "Ushimado Seto," emphasizes the region's political importance during its construction. The site is protected as a Setouchi City Historic Site. == Mound area == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 85 meters * Rear section: ''' Diameter: Approximately 50 meters ''' Height: Approximately 7-8 meters * Front section: ''' Length: Approximately 35 meters ''' Width: Approximately 25-30 meters ''' Height: Approximately 4 meters The 80-meter-class size identifies it as belonging to the highest-ranking leaders of the Yoshii River's eastern coastal region. The stone slabs presumed to be part of the burial chamber were sourced from {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島}} in Kagawa Prefecture. View of the mound (left: front section, back-right: rear section) | alternate_name = | affiliation = | location = {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓}} | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 37 | latitude_sec = 0.87 | longitude_deg = 134 | longitude_min = 9 | longitude_sec = 56.77 | iso = JP-33 | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | size = Length: 85 m
Height: 8 m (rear section) | burial_facilities = (Estimated) {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} | artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments | satellite_tombs = | built = Mid-to-late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} | buried_individual = | mausoleum = | historic_site = Designated Setouchi City Historic Site ("Tenjinyama Kofun") | tangible_cultural_property = | remarks = | map = {{location map|Japan Okayama|lat_deg=34|lat_min=37|lat_sec=0.87|lon_deg=134|lon_min=9|lon_sec=56.77|label= Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} }} [[File:Ushimado-bay viewed from Ushimado Olive Garden-3.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|location of the Kofun (lower left-center)}}{{small|Viewed from the {{ill|Ushimado Olive Garden|ja|牛窓オリーブ園}}. The far-right island is {{ill|Kuroshima|ja|黒島}}, where {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳}} is located. To the left of Kuroshima is {{ill|Maeshima (Okayama)|ja|前島}}, with {{ill|Shodoshima|ja|小豆島}} visible in the far background. The sea between the mound and Maeshima is known as Karakoto Seto or Ushimado Seto.}]]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300| height =250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | label1=Ushimado Tenjinyama | label-pos1=bottom | label-color1=black | mark1=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.svg | mark-size1=25 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | label2=Kuroshima | label-pos2=top | label-color2=black | mark2=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.svg | mark-size2=25 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | label3=Kahoyama | label-pos3=bottom | label-color3=black | mark3=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.svg | mark-size3=25 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | label4=(Hakayama) | label-pos4=bottom | label-color4=black | mark4=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.svg | mark-size4=25 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | label5=Futazukayama | label-pos5=right | label-color5=black | mark5=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.svg | mark-size5=25 }} '''Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun''' (牛窓天神山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓}}. It is designated as a Setouchi City Historic Site under the name "Tenjinyama Kofun." == Overview == This Kofun is located on the ridge of a hill at the eastern edge of Ushimado Bay, southeast Okayama Prefecture.[http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/kurashi/kankorekishi/rekishi/jyuyobunkazai/shisekikoukou/shijyuyo/1418099490778.html Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun] (Setouchi City website). Ushimado Bay contains five keyhole-shaped Kofuns: this mound, {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}, {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳}}. Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun is the oldest among them.On-site explanation board. The Ushimado Tenjin Shrine is located on its southern side. No formal excavations have been conducted. The mound has a primitive keyhole type, with its front section undeveloped and flat, oriented south toward the sea. The rear section is believed to have two tiers, while the front section has one. Surface features include stone cover (rounded cobblestones) and haniwa fragments (cylindrical and jar-shaped). The burial facility is estimated to be a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, with slab-like stones visible at the rear section's summit. Artifacts have not been clearly identified. The construction is estimated to date to the mid-to-late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the early Kofun period. Its strategic placement overlooking the sea passage between Ushimado Bay and Maeshima, known as "Karakoto Seto" or "Ushimado Seto," emphasizes the region's political importance during its construction. The site is protected as a Setouchi City Historic Site. == Mound area == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 85 meters * Rear section: ''' Diameter: Approximately 50 meters ''' Height: Approximately 7-8 meters * Front section: ''' Length: Approximately 35 meters ''' Width: Approximately 25-30 meters ''' Height: Approximately 4 meters The 80-meter-class size identifies it as belonging to the highest-ranking leaders of the Yoshii River's eastern coastal region. The stone slabs presumed to be part of the burial chamber were sourced from {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島}} in Kagawa Prefecture. File:Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun, funcho.jpg|Summit of the rear section File:Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View of the rear section from the front File:Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View of the front section from the rear File:Ushimado Tenjinja, haiden.jpg|Ushimado Tenjin Shrine, located on the southern side == other_designation == === Setouchi City Designated other_designation === * Historic Site ''' Tenjinyama Kofun{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/91/bunkazai_itiran(20161001).pdf Designated other_designation List]}} (Setouchi City website). == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not used in this article)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|title=Okayama Prefecture Archaeological Materials Vol. 18|publisher=Okayama Prefecture|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun}} * It is designated as a Setouchi City Historic Site under the name "Tenjinyama Kofun." == Overview == This Kofun is located on the ridge of a hill at the eastern edge of Ushimado Bay, southeast Okayama Prefecture.[http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/kurashi/kankorekishi/rekishi/jyuyobunkazai/shisekikoukou/shijyuyo/1418099490778.html Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun] (Setouchi City website). Ushimado Bay contains five keyhole-shaped Kofuns: this mound, {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}, {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳}}. Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun is the oldest among them.On-site explanation board. The Ushimado Tenjin Shrine is located on its southern side. No formal excavations have been conducted. The mound has a primitive keyhole type, with its front section undeveloped and flat, oriented south toward the sea. The rear section is believed to have two tiers, while the front section has one. Surface features include stone cover (rounded cobblestones) and haniwa fragments (cylindrical and jar-shaped). The burial facility is estimated to be a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, with slab-like stones visible at the rear section's summit. Artifacts have not been clearly identified. The construction is estimated to date to the mid-to-late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the early Kofun period. Its strategic placement overlooking the sea passage between Ushimado Bay and Maeshima, known as "Karakoto Seto" or "Ushimado Seto," emphasizes the region's political importance during its construction. The site is protected as a Setouchi City Historic Site. == Mound area == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 85 meters * Rear section: ''' Diameter: Approximately 50 meters ''' Height: Approximately 7-8 meters * Front section: ''' Length: Approximately 35 meters ''' Width: Approximately 25-30 meters ''' Height: Approximately 4 meters The 80-meter-class size identifies it as belonging to the highest-ranking leaders of the Yoshii River's eastern coastal region. The stone slabs presumed to be part of the burial chamber were sourced from {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島}} in Kagawa Prefecture. The sea between the mound and Maeshima is known as Karakoto Seto or Ushimado Seto.}]]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300| height =250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | label1=Ushimado Tenjinyama | label-pos1=bottom | label-color1=black | mark1=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.svg | mark-size1=25 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | label2=Kuroshima | label-pos2=top | label-color2=black | mark2=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.svg | mark-size2=25 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | label3=Kahoyama | label-pos3=bottom | label-color3=black | mark3=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.svg | mark-size3=25 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | label4=(Hakayama) | label-pos4=bottom | label-color4=black | mark4=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.svg | mark-size4=25 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | label5=Futazukayama | label-pos5=right | label-color5=black | mark5=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.svg | mark-size5=25 }} '''Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun''' (牛窓天神山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓}}. It is designated as a Setouchi City Historic Site under the name "Tenjinyama Kofun." == Overview == This Kofun is located on the ridge of a hill at the eastern edge of Ushimado Bay, southeast Okayama Prefecture.[http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/kurashi/kankorekishi/rekishi/jyuyobunkazai/shisekikoukou/shijyuyo/1418099490778.html Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun] (Setouchi City website). Ushimado Bay contains five keyhole-shaped Kofuns: this mound, {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}, {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳}}. Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun is the oldest among them.On-site explanation board. The Ushimado Tenjin Shrine is located on its southern side. No formal excavations have been conducted. The mound has a primitive keyhole type, with its front section undeveloped and flat, oriented south toward the sea. The rear section is believed to have two tiers, while the front section has one. Surface features include stone cover (rounded cobblestones) and haniwa fragments (cylindrical and jar-shaped). The burial facility is estimated to be a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, with slab-like stones visible at the rear section's summit. Artifacts have not been clearly identified. The construction is estimated to date to the mid-to-late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the early Kofun period. Its strategic placement overlooking the sea passage between Ushimado Bay and Maeshima, known as "Karakoto Seto" or "Ushimado Seto," emphasizes the region's political importance during its construction. The site is protected as a Setouchi City Historic Site. == Mound area == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 85 meters * Rear section: ''' Diameter: Approximately 50 meters ''' Height: Approximately 7-8 meters * Front section: ''' Length: Approximately 35 meters ''' Width: Approximately 25-30 meters ''' Height: Approximately 4 meters The 80-meter-class size identifies it as belonging to the highest-ranking leaders of the Yoshii River's eastern coastal region. The stone slabs presumed to be part of the burial chamber were sourced from {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島}} in Kagawa Prefecture. File:Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun, funcho.jpg|Summit of the rear section File:Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View of the rear section from the front File:Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View of the front section from the rear File:Ushimado Tenjinja, haiden.jpg|Ushimado Tenjin Shrine, located on the southern side == other_designation == === Setouchi City Designated It is designated as a Setouchi City Historic Site under the name "Tenjinyama Kofun." == Overview == This Kofun is located on the ridge of a hill at the eastern edge of Ushimado Bay, southeast Okayama Prefecture.[http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/kurashi/kankorekishi/rekishi/jyuyobunkazai/shisekikoukou/shijyuyo/1418099490778.html Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun] (Setouchi City website). Ushimado Bay contains five keyhole-shaped Kofuns: this mound, {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}, {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳}}. Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun is the oldest among them.On-site explanation board. The Ushimado Tenjin Shrine is located on its southern side. No formal excavations have been conducted. The mound has a primitive keyhole type, with its front section undeveloped and flat, oriented south toward the sea. The rear section is believed to have two tiers, while the front section has one. Surface features include stone cover (rounded cobblestones) and haniwa fragments (cylindrical and jar-shaped). The burial facility is estimated to be a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, with slab-like stones visible at the rear section's summit. Artifacts have not been clearly identified. The construction is estimated to date to the mid-to-late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the early Kofun period. Its strategic placement overlooking the sea passage between Ushimado Bay and Maeshima, known as "Karakoto Seto" or "Ushimado Seto," emphasizes the region's political importance during its construction. The site is protected as a Setouchi City Historic Site. == Mound area == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 85 meters * Rear section: ''' Diameter: Approximately 50 meters ''' Height: Approximately 7-8 meters * Front section: ''' Length: Approximately 35 meters ''' Width: Approximately 25-30 meters ''' Height: Approximately 4 meters The 80-meter-class size identifies it as belonging to the highest-ranking leaders of the Yoshii River's eastern coastal region. The stone slabs presumed to be part of the burial chamber were sourced from {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島}} in Kagawa Prefecture. It is designated as a Setouchi City other_designation under the name "Tenjinyama Kofun." == Overview == This Kofun is located on the ridge of a hill at the eastern edge of Ushimado Bay, southeast Okayama Prefecture.[http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/kurashi/kankorekishi/rekishi/jyuyobunkazai/shisekikoukou/shijyuyo/1418099490778.html Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun] (Setouchi City website). Ushimado Bay contains five keyhole-shaped Kofuns: this mound, {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}, {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳}}. Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun is the oldest among them.On-site explanation board. The Ushimado Tenjin Shrine is located on its southern side. No formal excavations have been conducted. The mound has a primitive keyhole type, with its front section undeveloped and flat, oriented south toward the sea. The rear section is believed to have two tiers, while the front section has one. Surface features include stone cover (rounded cobblestones) and haniwa fragments (cylindrical and jar-shaped). The burial facility is estimated to be a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, with slab-like stones visible at the rear section's summit. Artifacts have not been clearly identified. The construction is estimated to date to the mid-to-late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the early Kofun period. Its strategic placement overlooking the sea passage between Ushimado Bay and Maeshima, known as "Karakoto Seto" or "Ushimado Seto," emphasizes the region's political importance during its construction. The site is protected as a Setouchi City other_designation. == Mound area == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 85 meters * Rear section: ''' Diameter: Approximately 50 meters ''' Height: Approximately 7-8 meters * Front section: ''' Length: Approximately 35 meters ''' Width: Approximately 25-30 meters ''' Height: Approximately 4 meters The 80-meter-class size identifies it as belonging to the highest-ranking leaders of the Yoshii River's eastern coastal region. The stone slabs presumed to be part of the burial chamber were sourced from {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島}} in Kagawa Prefecture. The sea between the mound and Maeshima is known as Karakoto Seto or Ushimado Seto.}]]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300| height =250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | label1=Ushimado Tenjinyama | label-pos1=bottom | label-color1=black | mark1=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.svg | mark-size1=25 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | label2=Kuroshima | label-pos2=top | label-color2=black | mark2=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.svg | mark-size2=25 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | label3=Kahoyama | label-pos3=bottom | label-color3=black | mark3=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.svg | mark-size3=25 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | label4=(Hakayama) | label-pos4=bottom | label-color4=black | mark4=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.svg | mark-size4=25 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | label5=Futazukayama | label-pos5=right | label-color5=black | mark5=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.svg | mark-size5=25 }} '''Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun''' (牛窓天神山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓}}. It is designated as a Setouchi City other_designation under the name "Tenjinyama Kofun." == Overview == This Kofun is located on the ridge of a hill at the eastern edge of Ushimado Bay, southeast Okayama Prefecture.[http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/kurashi/kankorekishi/rekishi/jyuyobunkazai/shisekikoukou/shijyuyo/1418099490778.html Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun] (Setouchi City website). Ushimado Bay contains five keyhole-shaped Kofuns: this mound, {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}, {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳}}. Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun is the oldest among them.On-site explanation board. The Ushimado Tenjin Shrine is located on its southern side. No formal excavations have been conducted. The mound has a primitive keyhole type, with its front section undeveloped and flat, oriented south toward the sea. The rear section is believed to have two tiers, while the front section has one. Surface features include stone cover (rounded cobblestones) and haniwa fragments (cylindrical and jar-shaped). The burial facility is estimated to be a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, with slab-like stones visible at the rear section's summit. Artifacts have not been clearly identified. The construction is estimated to date to the mid-to-late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the early Kofun period. Its strategic placement overlooking the sea passage between Ushimado Bay and Maeshima, known as "Karakoto Seto" or "Ushimado Seto," emphasizes the region's political importance during its construction. The site is protected as a Setouchi City other_designation. == Mound area == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 85 meters * Rear section: ''' Diameter: Approximately 50 meters ''' Height: Approximately 7-8 meters * Front section: ''' Length: Approximately 35 meters ''' Width: Approximately 25-30 meters ''' Height: Approximately 4 meters The 80-meter-class size identifies it as belonging to the highest-ranking leaders of the Yoshii River's eastern coastal region. The stone slabs presumed to be part of the burial chamber were sourced from {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島}} in Kagawa Prefecture. Ushimado Bay contains five keyhole-shaped Kofuns: this mound, {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}, {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳}}. Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun is the oldest among them.On-site explanation board. The Ushimado Tenjin Shrine is located on its southern side. No formal excavations have been conducted. The burial facility is estimated to be a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, with slab-like stones visible at the rear section's summit. Artifacts have not been clearly identified. The construction is estimated to date to the mid-to-late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the early Kofun period. Its strategic placement overlooking the sea passage between Ushimado Bay and Maeshima, known as "Karakoto Seto" or "Ushimado Seto," emphasizes the region's political importance during its construction. The stone slabs presumed to be part of the burial chamber were sourced from {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島}} in Kagawa Prefecture. The sea between the mound and Maeshima is known as Karakoto Seto or Ushimado Seto.}]]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300| height =250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | label1=Ushimado Tenjinyama | label-pos1=bottom | label-color1=black | mark1=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.svg | mark-size1=25 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | label2=Kuroshima | label-pos2=top | label-color2=black | mark2=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.svg | mark-size2=25 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | label3=Kahoyama | label-pos3=bottom | label-color3=black | mark3=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.svg | mark-size3=25 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | label4=(Hakayama) | label-pos4=bottom | label-color4=black | mark4=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.svg | mark-size4=25 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | label5=Futazukayama | label-pos5=right | label-color5=black | mark5=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.svg | mark-size5=25 }} '''Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun''' (牛窓天神山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓}}. It is designated as a Setouchi City other_designation under the name "Tenjinyama Kofun." == Overview == This Kofun is located on the ridge of a hill at the eastern edge of Ushimado Bay, southeast Okayama Prefecture.[http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/kurashi/kankorekishi/rekishi/jyuyobunkazai/shisekikoukou/shijyuyo/1418099490778.html Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun] (Setouchi City website). Ushimado Bay contains five keyhole-shaped Kofuns: this mound, {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}, {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳}}. Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun is the oldest among them.On-site explanation board. The Ushimado Tenjin Shrine is located on its southern side. No formal excavations have been conducted. The mound has a primitive keyhole type, with its front section undeveloped and flat, oriented south toward the sea. The rear section is believed to have two tiers, while the front section has one. Surface features include stone cover (rounded cobblestones) and haniwa fragments (cylindrical and jar-shaped). The burial facility is estimated to be a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, with slab-like stones visible at the rear section's summit. burial goods have not been clearly identified. The construction is estimated to date to the mid-to-late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the early Kofun period. Its strategic placement overlooking the sea passage between Ushimado Bay and Maeshima, known as "Karakoto Seto" or "Ushimado Seto," emphasizes the region's political importance during its construction. The site is protected as a Setouchi City other_designation. == Mound area == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 85 meters * Rear section: ''' Diameter: Approximately 50 meters ''' Height: Approximately 7-8 meters * Front section: ''' Length: Approximately 35 meters ''' Width: Approximately 25-30 meters ''' Height: Approximately 4 meters The 80-meter-class size identifies it as belonging to the highest-ranking leaders of the Yoshii River's eastern coastal region. The stone slabs presumed to be part of the burial chamber were sourced from {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島}} in Kagawa Prefecture. File:Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun, funcho.jpg|Summit of the rear section File:Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View of the rear section from the front File:Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View of the front section from the rear File:Ushimado Tenjinja, haiden.jpg|Ushimado Tenjin Shrine, located on the southern side == other_designation == === Setouchi City Designated It is designation1 as a Setouchi City other_designation under the name "Tenjinyama Kofun." == Overview == This Kofun is located on the ridge of a hill at the eastern edge of Ushimado Bay, southeast Okayama Prefecture.[http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/kurashi/kankorekishi/rekishi/jyuyobunkazai/shisekikoukou/shijyuyo/1418099490778.html Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun] (Setouchi City website). Ushimado Bay contains five keyhole-shaped Kofuns: this mound, {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}, {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳}}. Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun is the oldest among them.On-site explanation board. The Ushimado Tenjin Shrine is located on its southern side. No formal excavations have been conducted. The mound has a primitive keyhole type, with its front section undeveloped and flat, oriented south toward the sea. The rear section is believed to have two tiers, while the front section has one. Surface features include stone cover (rounded cobblestones) and haniwa fragments (cylindrical and jar-shaped). The burial facility is estimated to be a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, with slab-like stones visible at the rear section's summit. burial goods have not been clearly identified. The construction is estimated to date to the mid-to-late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the early Kofun period. Its strategic placement overlooking the sea passage between Ushimado Bay and Maeshima, known as "Karakoto Seto" or "Ushimado Seto," emphasizes the region's political importance during its construction. The site is protected as a Setouchi City other_designation. == Mound area == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 85 meters * Rear section: ''' Diameter: Approximately 50 meters ''' Height: Approximately 7-8 meters * Front section: ''' Length: Approximately 35 meters ''' Width: Approximately 25-30 meters ''' Height: Approximately 4 meters The 80-meter-class size identifies it as belonging to the highest-ranking leaders of the Yoshii River's eastern coastal region. The stone slabs presumed to be part of the burial chamber were sourced from {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島}} in Kagawa Prefecture. It is designation1 as a Setouchi City other_designation under the name "Tenjinyama Kofun." == Overview == This Kofun is located on the ridge of a hill at the eastern edge of Ushimado Bay, southeast Okayama Prefecture.[http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/kurashi/kankorekishi/rekishi/jyuyobunkazai/shisekikoukou/shijyuyo/1418099490778.html Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun] (Setouchi City website). Ushimado Bay contains five keyhole-shaped Kofuns: this mound, {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}, {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳}}. Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun is the oldest among them.On-site explanation board. The Ushimado Tenjin Shrine is located on its southern side. No formal excavations have been conducted. The mound has a primitive keyhole type, with its front section undeveloped and flat, oriented south toward the sea. The rear section is believed to have two tiers, while the front section has one. Surface features include stone cover (rounded cobblestones) and haniwa fragments (cylindrical and jar-shaped). The burial facility is estimated to be a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, with slab-like stones visible at the rear section's summit. burial goods have not been clearly identified. The construction is estimated to date to the mid-to-late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the early Kofun period. Its strategic placement overlooking the sea passage between Ushimado Bay and Maeshima, known as "Karakoto Seto" or "Ushimado Seto," emphasizes the region's political importance during its construction. The site is protected as a Setouchi City other_designation. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 85 meters * Rear section: ''' Diameter: Approximately 50 meters ''' Height: Approximately 7-8 meters * Front section: ''' Length: Approximately 35 meters ''' Width: Approximately 25-30 meters ''' Height: Approximately 4 meters The 80-meter-class size identifies it as belonging to the highest-ranking leaders of the Yoshii River's eastern coastal region. The stone slabs presumed to be part of the burial chamber were sourced from {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島}} in Kagawa Prefecture. It is other_designation1 as a Setouchi City other_other_designation under the name "Tenjinyama Kofun." == Overview == This Kofun is located on the ridge of a hill at the eastern edge of Ushimado Bay, southeast Okayama Prefecture.[http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/kurashi/kankorekishi/rekishi/jyuyobunkazai/shisekikoukou/shijyuyo/1418099490778.html Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun] (Setouchi City website). Ushimado Bay contains five keyhole-shaped Kofuns: this mound, {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}, {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳}}. Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun is the oldest among them.On-site explanation board. The Ushimado Tenjin Shrine is located on its southern side. No typeal excavations have been conducted. The mound has a primitive keyhole type, with its front section undeveloped and flat, oriented south toward the sea. The rear section is believed to have two tiers, while the front section has one. Surface features include stone cover (rounded cobblestones) and haniwa fragments (cylindrical and jar-shaped). The burial facility is estimated to be a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, with slab-like stones visible at the rear section's summit. burial goods have not been clearly identified. The construction is estimated to date to the mid-to-late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the early Kofun period. Its strategic placement overlooking the sea passage between Ushimado Bay and Maeshima, known as "Karakoto Seto" or "Ushimado Seto," emphasizes the region's political importance during its construction. The site is protected as a Setouchi City other_other_designation. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 85 meters * Rear section: ''' Diameter: Approximately 50 meters ''' Height: Approximately 7-8 meters * Front section: ''' Length: Approximately 35 meters ''' Width: Approximately 25-30 meters ''' Height: Approximately 4 meters The 80-meter-class size identifies it as belonging to the highest-ranking leaders of the Yoshii River's eastern coastal region. The stone slabs presumed to be part of the burial chamber were sourced from {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島}} in Kagawa Prefecture. It is other_designation1 as a Setouchi City other_other_designation under the name "Tenjinyama Kofun." == Overview == This Kofun is located on the ridge of a hill at the eastern edge of Ushimado Bay, southeast Okayama Prefecture.[http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/kurashi/kankorekishi/rekishi/jyuyobunkazai/shisekikoukou/shijyuyo/1418099490778.html Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun] (Setouchi City website). Ushimado Bay contains five keyhole-shaped Kofuns: this mound, {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}, {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳}}. Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun is the oldest among them.On-site explanation board. The Ushimado Tenjin Shrine is located on its southern side. No typeal excavations have been conducted. The mound has a primitive keyhole type, with its front section undeveloped and flat, oriented south toward the sea. The rear section is believed to have two tiers, while the front section has one. Surface features include stone cover (rounded cobblestones) and haniwa fragments (cylindrical and jar-shaped). The burial facility is estimated to be a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, with slab-like stones visible at the rear section's summit. burial goods have not been clearly identified. The construction is estimated to date to the mid-to-late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the early Kofun period. Its strategic placement overlooking the sea passage between Ushimado Bay and Maeshima, known as "Karakoto Seto" or "Ushimado Seto," emphasizes the region's political importance during its construction. The site is protected as a Setouchi City other_other_designation. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 85 meters * Rear section: ''' Diameter: Approximately 50 meters ''' Height: Approximately 7-8 meters * Front section: ''' Length: Approximately 35 meters ''' Width: Approximately 25-30 meters ''' Height: Approximately 4 meters The 80-meter-class size identifies it as belonging to the highest-ranking leaders of the Yoshii River's eastern coastal region. The stone slabs presumed to be part of the burial chamber were sourced from {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島}} in Kagawa Prefecture. It is other_designation1 as a Setouchi City other_other_designation under the name "Tenjinyama Kofun." == Overview == This Kofun is located on the ridge of a hill at the eastern edge of Ushimado Bay, southeast Okayama Prefecture.[http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/kurashi/kankorekishi/rekishi/jyuyobunkazai/shisekikoukou/shijyuyo/1418099490778.html Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun] (Setouchi City website). Ushimado Bay contains five keyhole-shaped Kofuns: this mound, {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}, {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳}}. Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun is the oldest among them.On-site explanation board. The Ushimado Tenjin Shrine is located on its southern side. No typeal excavations have been conducted. The mound has a primitive keyhole type, with its front section undeveloped and flat, oriented south toward the sea. The rear section is believed to have two tiers, while the front section has one. Surface features include stone cover (rounded cobblestones) and haniwa fragments (cylindrical and jar-shaped). The burial facility is estimated to be a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, with slab-like stones visible at the rear section's summit. burial goods have not been clearly identified. The construction is estimated to date to the mid-to-late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the early Kofun period. Its strategic placement overlooking the sea passage between Ushimado Bay and Maeshima, known as "Karakoto Seto" or "Ushimado Seto," emphasizes the region's political importance during its construction. The site is protected as a Setouchi City other_other_designation. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 85 meters * Rear section: ''' Diameter: Approximately 50 meters ''' Height: Approximately 7-8 meters * Front section: ''' Length: Approximately 35 meters ''' Width: Approximately 25-30 meters ''' Height: Approximately 4 meters The 80-meter-class size identifies it as belonging to the highest-ranking leaders of the Yoshii River's eastern coastal region. The stone slabs presumed to be part of the burial chamber were sourced from {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島}} in Kagawa Prefecture. zenpoubu.jpg|View of the front section from the rear File:Ushimado Tenjinja, haiden.jpg|Ushimado Tenjin Shrine, located on the southern side == other_other_designation == === Setouchi City Designated It is other_designation1 as a Setouchi City other_other_designation under the name "Tenjinyama Kofun." == Overview == This Kofun is located on the ridge of a hill at the eastern edge of Ushimado Bay, southeast Okayama Prefecture.[http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/kurashi/kankorekishi/rekishi/jyuyobunkazai/shisekikoukou/shijyuyo/1418099490778.html Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun] (Setouchi City website). Ushimado Bay contains five keyhole-shaped Kofuns: this mound, {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}, {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳}}. Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun is the oldest among them.On-site explanation board. The Ushimado Tenjin Shrine is located on its southern side. No typeal excavations have been conducted. The mound has a primitive keyhole type, with its front section undeveloped and flat, oriented south toward the sea. The rear section is believed to have two tiers, while the front section has one. Surface features include stone cover (rounded cobblestones) and haniwa fragments (cylindrical and jar-shaped). The burial facility is estimated to be a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, with slab-like stones visible at the rear section's summit. burial goods have not been clearly identified. The construction is estimated to date to the mid-to-late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the early Kofun period. Its strategic placement overlooking the sea passage between Ushimado Bay and Maeshima, known as "Karakoto Seto" or "Ushimado Seto," emphasizes the region's political importance during its construction. The site is protected as a Setouchi City other_other_designation. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 85 meters * Rear section: ''' Diameter: Approximately 50 meters ''' Height: Approximately 7-8 meters * Front section: ''' Length: Approximately 35 meters ''' Width: Approximately 25-30 meters ''' Height: Approximately 4 meters The 80-meter-class size identifies it as belonging to the highest-ranking leaders of the Yoshii River's eastern coastal region. The stone slabs presumed to be part of the burial chamber were sourced from {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島}} in Kagawa Prefecture. It is other_designation1 as a Setouchi City other_designation under the name "Tenjinyama Kofun." == Overview == This Kofun is located on the ridge of a hill at the eastern edge of Ushimado Bay, southeast Okayama Prefecture.[http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/kurashi/kankorekishi/rekishi/jyuyobunkazai/shisekikoukou/shijyuyo/1418099490778.html Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun] (Setouchi City website). Ushimado Bay contains five keyhole-shaped Kofuns: this mound, {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}, {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳}}. Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun is the oldest among them.On-site explanation board. The Ushimado Tenjin Shrine is located on its southern side. No typeal excavations have been conducted. The mound has a primitive keyhole type, with its front section undeveloped and flat, oriented south toward the sea. The rear section is believed to have two tiers, while the front section has one. Surface features include stone cover (rounded cobblestones) and haniwa fragments (cylindrical and jar-shaped). The burial facility is estimated to be a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, with slab-like stones visible at the rear section's summit. burial goods have not been clearly identified. The construction is estimated to date to the mid-to-late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the early Kofun period. Its strategic placement overlooking the sea passage between Ushimado Bay and Maeshima, known as "Karakoto Seto" or "Ushimado Seto," emphasizes the region's political importance during its construction. The site is protected as a Setouchi City other_designation. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 85 meters * Rear section: ''' Diameter: Approximately 50 meters ''' Height: Approximately 7-8 meters * Front section: ''' Length: Approximately 35 meters ''' Width: Approximately 25-30 meters ''' Height: Approximately 4 meters The 80-meter-class size identifies it as belonging to the highest-ranking leaders of the Yoshii River's eastern coastal region. The stone slabs presumed to be part of the burial chamber were sourced from {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島}} in Kagawa Prefecture. zenpoubu.jpg|View of the front section from the rear File:Ushimado Tenjinja, haiden.jpg|Ushimado Tenjin Shrine, located on the southern side == other_designation == === Setouchi City Designated Height: 8 m (rear section) | burial_facilities = (Estimated) {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} | burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments | satellite_tombs = | built = Mid-to-late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} | buried_individual = | mausoleum = | historic_site = Designated Setouchi City other_designation ("Tenjinyama Kofun") | tangible_cultural_property = | remarks = | map = {{location map|Japan Okayama|lat_deg=34|lat_min=37|lat_sec=0.87|lon_deg=134|lon_min=9|lon_sec=56.77|map_dot_label= Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} }} [[File:Ushimado-bay viewed from Ushimado Olive Garden-3.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|location of the Kofun (lower left-center)}}{{small|Viewed from the {{ill|Ushimado Olive Garden|ja|牛窓オリーブ園}}. The far-right island is {{ill|Kuroshima|ja|黒島}}, where {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳}} is located. To the left of Kuroshima is {{ill|Maeshima (Okayama)|ja|前島}}, with {{ill|Shodoshima|ja|小豆島}} visible in the far background. The sea between the mound and Maeshima is known as Karakoto Seto or Ushimado Seto.}]]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300| height =250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1=Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1=bottom | map_dot_label-color1=black | mark1=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.svg | mark-size1=25 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2=Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2=top | map_dot_label-color2=black | mark2=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.svg | mark-size2=25 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3=Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3=bottom | map_dot_label-color3=black | mark3=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.svg | mark-size3=25 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4=(Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4=bottom | map_dot_label-color4=black | mark4=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.svg | mark-size4=25 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5=Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5=right | map_dot_label-color5=black | mark5=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.svg | mark-size5=25 }} '''Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun''' (牛窓天神山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓}}. It is other_designation1 as a Setouchi City other_designation under the name "Tenjinyama Kofun." == Overview == This Kofun is located on the ridge of a hill at the eastern edge of Ushimado Bay, southeast Okayama Prefecture.[http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/kurashi/kankorekishi/rekishi/jyuyobunkazai/shisekikoukou/shijyuyo/1418099490778.html Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun] (Setouchi City website). Ushimado Bay contains five keyhole-shaped Kofuns: this mound, {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}, {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳}}. Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun is the oldest among them.On-site explanation board. The Ushimado Tenjin Shrine is located on its southern side. No typeal excavations have been conducted. The mound has a primitive keyhole type, with its front section undeveloped and flat, oriented south toward the sea. The rear section is believed to have two tiers, while the front section has one. Surface features include stone cover (rounded cobblestones) and haniwa fragments (cylindrical and jar-shaped). The burial facility is estimated to be a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, with slab-like stones visible at the rear section's summit. burial goods have not been clearly identified. The construction is estimated to date to the mid-to-late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the early Kofun period. Its strategic placement overlooking the sea passage between Ushimado Bay and Maeshima, known as "Karakoto Seto" or "Ushimado Seto," emphasizes the region's political importance during its construction. The site is protected as a Setouchi City other_designation. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 85 meters * Rear section: ''' Diameter: Approximately 50 meters ''' Height: Approximately 7-8 meters * Front section: ''' Length: Approximately 35 meters ''' Width: Approximately 25-30 meters ''' Height: Approximately 4 meters The 80-meter-class size identifies it as belonging to the highest-ranking leaders of the Yoshii River's eastern coastal region. The stone slabs presumed to be part of the burial chamber were sourced from {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島}} in Kagawa Prefecture. File:Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun, funcho.jpg|Summit of the rear section File:Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View of the rear section from the front File:Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View of the front section from the rear File:Ushimado Tenjinja, haiden.jpg|Ushimado Tenjin Shrine, located on the southern side == other_designation == === Setouchi City Designated other_designation === * other_designation ''' Tenjinyama Kofun{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/91/bunkazai_itiran(20161001).pdf Designated other_designation List]}} (Setouchi City website). == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not used in this article)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|title=Okayama Prefecture Archaeological Materials Vol. 18|publisher=Okayama Prefecture|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun}} * But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. The sea between the mound and Maeshima is known as Karakoto Seto or Ushimado Seto.}]]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300| height =250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1=Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1=bottom | map_dot_label-color1=black | mark1=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.svg | mark-size1=25 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2=Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2=top | map_dot_label-color2=black | mark2=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.svg | mark-size2=25 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3=Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3=bottom | map_dot_label-color3=black | mark3=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.svg | mark-size3=25 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4=(Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4=bottom | map_dot_label-color4=black | mark4=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.svg | mark-size4=25 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5=Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5=right | map_dot_label-color5=black | mark5=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.svg | mark-size5=25 }} '''Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun''' (牛窓天神山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓}}. It is other_designation1 as a Setouchi City other_designation under the name "Tenjinyama Kofun." == Overview == This Kofun is located on the ridge of a hill at the eastern edge of Ushimado Bay, southeast Okayama Prefecture.[http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/kurashi/kankorekishi/rekishi/jyuyobunkazai/shisekikoukou/shijyuyo/1418099490778.html Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun] (Setouchi City website). Ushimado Bay contains five keyhole-shaped Kofuns: this mound, {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}, {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳}}. Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun is the oldest among them.On-site explanation board. The Ushimado Tenjin Shrine is located on its southern side. No typeal excavations have been conducted. The mound has a primitive keyhole type, with its front section undeveloped and flat, oriented south toward the sea. The rear section is believed to have two tiers, while the front section has one. Surface features include stone cover (rounded cobblestones) and haniwa fragments (cylindrical and jar-shaped). The burial facility is estimated to be a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, with slab-like stones visible at the rear section's summit. burial goods have not been clearly identified. The construction is estimated to date to the mid-to-late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the early Kofun period. Its strategic placement overlooking the sea passage between Ushimado Bay and Maeshima, known as "Karakoto Seto" or "Ushimado Seto," emphasizes the region's political importance during its construction. The site is protected as a Setouchi City other_designation. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 85 meters * Rear section: ''' Diameter: Approximately 50 meters ''' Height: Approximately 7-8 meters * Front section: ''' Length: Approximately 35 meters ''' Width: Approximately 25-30 meters ''' Height: Approximately 4 meters The 80-meter-class size identifies it as belonging to the highest-ranking leaders of the Yoshii River's eastern coastal region. The stone slabs presumed to be part of the burial chamber were sourced from {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島}} in Kagawa Prefecture. Height: 8 m (rear section) | burial_facilities = (Estimated) {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} | burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments | satellite_tombs = | built = Mid-to-late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} | buried_individual = | mausoleum = | historic_site = Designated Setouchi City other_designation ("Tenjinyama Kofun") | tangible_notes = | remarks = | map = {{location map|Japan Okayama|lat_deg=34|lat_min=37|lat_sec=0.87|lon_deg=134|lon_min=9|lon_sec=56.77|map_dot_label= Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} }} [[File:Ushimado-bay viewed from Ushimado Olive Garden-3.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|location of the Kofun (lower left-center)}}{{small|Viewed from the {{ill|Ushimado Olive Garden|ja|牛窓オリーブ園}}. The far-right island is {{ill|Kuroshima|ja|黒島}}, where {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳}} is located. To the left of Kuroshima is {{ill|Maeshima (Okayama)|ja|前島}}, with {{ill|Shodoshima|ja|小豆島}} visible in the far background. The sea between the mound and Maeshima is known as Karakoto Seto or Ushimado Seto.}]]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300| height =250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1=Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1=bottom | map_dot_label-color1=black | mark1=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.svg | mark-size1=25 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2=Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2=top | map_dot_label-color2=black | mark2=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.svg | mark-size2=25 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3=Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3=bottom | map_dot_label-color3=black | mark3=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.svg | mark-size3=25 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4=(Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4=bottom | map_dot_label-color4=black | mark4=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.svg | mark-size4=25 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5=Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5=right | map_dot_label-color5=black | mark5=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.svg | mark-size5=25 }} '''Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun''' (牛窓天神山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓}}. It is other_designation1 as a Setouchi City other_designation under the name "Tenjinyama Kofun." == Overview == This Kofun is located on the ridge of a hill at the eastern edge of Ushimado Bay, southeast Okayama Prefecture.[http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/kurashi/kankorekishi/rekishi/jyuyobunkazai/shisekikoukou/shijyuyo/1418099490778.html Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun] (Setouchi City website). Ushimado Bay contains five keyhole-shaped Kofuns: this mound, {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}, {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳}}. Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun is the oldest among them.On-site explanation board. The Ushimado Tenjin Shrine is located on its southern side. No typeal excavations have been conducted. The mound has a primitive keyhole type, with its front section undeveloped and flat, oriented south toward the sea. The rear section is believed to have two tiers, while the front section has one. Surface features include stone cover (rounded cobblestones) and haniwa fragments (cylindrical and jar-shaped). The burial facility is estimated to be a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, with slab-like stones visible at the rear section's summit. burial goods have not been clearly identified. The construction is estimated to date to the mid-to-late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the early Kofun period. Its strategic placement overlooking the sea passage between Ushimado Bay and Maeshima, known as "Karakoto Seto" or "Ushimado Seto," emphasizes the region's political importance during its construction. The site is protected as a Setouchi City other_designation. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 85 meters * Rear section: ''' Diameter: Approximately 50 meters ''' Height: Approximately 7-8 meters * Front section: ''' Length: Approximately 35 meters ''' Width: Approximately 25-30 meters ''' Height: Approximately 4 meters The 80-meter-class size identifies it as belonging to the highest-ranking leaders of the Yoshii River's eastern coastal region. The stone slabs presumed to be part of the burial chamber were sourced from {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島}} in Kagawa Prefecture. File:Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun, funcho.jpg|Summit of the rear section File:Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View of the rear section from the front File:Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View of the front section from the rear File:Ushimado Tenjinja, haiden.jpg|Ushimado Tenjin Shrine, located on the southern side == other_designation == === Setouchi City Designated other_designation === * other_designation ''' Tenjinyama Kofun{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/91/bunkazai_itiran(20161001).pdf Designated other_designation List]}} (Setouchi City website). == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not used in this article)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|title=Okayama Prefecture Archaeological Materials Vol. 18|publisher=Okayama Prefecture|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun}} * Ushimado Bay contains five keyhole-shaped Kofuns: this mound, {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}, {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳}}. The mound has a primitive keyhole type, with its front section undeveloped and flat, oriented south toward the sea. The rear section is believed to have two tiers, while the front section has one. Surface features include stone cover (rounded cobblestones) and haniwa fragments (cylindrical and jar-shaped). The burial facility is estimated to be a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, with slab-like stones visible at the rear section's summit. burial goods have not been clearly identified. The construction is estimated to date to the mid-to-late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the early Kofun period. Its strategic placement overlooking the sea passage between Ushimado Bay and Maeshima, known as "Karakoto Seto" or "Ushimado Seto," emphasizes the region's political importance during its construction. The site is protected as a Setouchi City other_designation. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 85 meters * Rear section: ''' Diameter: Approximately 50 meters ''' Height: Approximately 7-8 meters * Front section: ''' Length: Approximately 35 meters ''' Width: Approximately 25-30 meters ''' Height: Approximately 4 meters The 80-meter-class size identifies it as belonging to the highest-ranking leaders of the Yoshii River's eastern coastal region. The stone slabs presumed to be part of the burial chamber were sourced from {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島}} in Kagawa Prefecture. zenpoubu.jpg|View of the front section from the rear File:Ushimado Tenjinja, haiden.jpg|Ushimado Tenjin Shrine, located on the southern side == other_designation == === Setouchi City Designated revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun | image = [[File:Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
View of the mound (left: front section, back-right: rear section) | alternate_name = | affiliation = | location = {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓}} | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 37 | coordinates = 0.87 | coordinates = 134 | coordinates = 9 | coordinates = 56.77 | iso = JP-33 | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | size = Length: 85 m
Height: 8 m (rear section) | burial_facilities = (Estimated) {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} | burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments | satellite_tombs = | built = Mid-to-late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} | buried_individual = | mausoleum = | historic_site = Designated Setouchi City other_designation ("Tenjinyama Kofun") | tangible_notes = | remarks = | map = {{location map|Japan Okayama|lat_deg=34|lat_min=37|lat_sec=0.87|lon_deg=134|lon_min=9|lon_sec=56.77|map_dot_label= Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} }} [[File:Ushimado-bay viewed from Ushimado Olive Garden-3.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|location of the Kofun (lower left-center)}}{{small|Viewed from the {{ill|Ushimado Olive Garden|ja|牛窓オリーブ園}}. The far-right island is {{ill|Kuroshima|ja|黒島}}, where {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳}} is located. To the left of Kuroshima is {{ill|Maeshima (Okayama)|ja|前島}}, with {{ill|Shodoshima|ja|小豆島}} visible in the far background. The sea between the mound and Maeshima is known as Karakoto Seto or Ushimado Seto.}]]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300| height =250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1=Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1=bottom | map_dot_label-color1=black | mark1=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.svg | mark-size1=25 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2=Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2=top | map_dot_label-color2=black | mark2=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.svg | mark-size2=25 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3=Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3=bottom | map_dot_label-color3=black | mark3=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.svg | mark-size3=25 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4=(Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4=bottom | map_dot_label-color4=black | mark4=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.svg | mark-size4=25 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5=Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5=right | map_dot_label-color5=black | mark5=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.svg | mark-size5=25 }} '''Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun''' (牛窓天神山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓}}. It is other_designation1 as a Setouchi City other_designation under the name "Tenjinyama Kofun." == Overview == This Kofun is located on the ridge of a hill at the eastern edge of Ushimado Bay, southeast Okayama Prefecture.[http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/kurashi/kankorekishi/rekishi/jyuyobunkazai/shisekikoukou/shijyuyo/1418099490778.html Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun] (Setouchi City website). Ushimado Bay contains five keyhole-shaped Kofuns: this mound, {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}, {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳}}. Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun is the oldest among them.On-site explanation board. The Ushimado Tenjin Shrine is located on its southern side. No typeal excavations have been conducted. The mound has a primitive keyhole type, with its front section undeveloped and flat, oriented south toward the sea. The rear section is believed to have two tiers, while the front section has one. Surface features include stone cover (rounded cobblestones) and haniwa fragments (cylindrical and jar-shaped). The burial facility is estimated to be a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, with slab-like stones visible at the rear section's summit. burial goods have not been clearly identified. The construction is estimated to date to the mid-to-late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the early Kofun period. Its strategic placement overlooking the sea passage between Ushimado Bay and Maeshima, known as "Karakoto Seto" or "Ushimado Seto," emphasizes the region's political importance during its construction. The site is protected as a Setouchi City other_designation. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 85 meters * Rear section: ''' Diameter: Approximately 50 meters ''' Height: Approximately 7-8 meters * Front section: ''' Length: Approximately 35 meters ''' Width: Approximately 25-30 meters ''' Height: Approximately 4 meters The 80-meter-class size identifies it as belonging to the highest-ranking leaders of the Yoshii River's eastern coastal region. The stone slabs presumed to be part of the burial chamber were sourced from {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島}} in Kagawa Prefecture. File:Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun, funcho.jpg|Summit of the rear section File:Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View of the rear section from the front File:Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View of the front section from the rear File:Ushimado Tenjinja, haiden.jpg|Ushimado Tenjin Shrine, located on the southern side == other_designation == === Setouchi City Designated other_designation === * other_designation ''' Tenjinyama Kofun{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/91/bunkazai_itiran(20161001).pdf Designated other_designation List]}} (Setouchi City website). == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not used in this article)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|title=Okayama Prefecture Archaeological Materials Vol. 18|publisher=Okayama Prefecture|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun}} * It is other_designation1 as a Setouchi City other_designation under the name "Tenjinyama Kofun." == Overview == This Kofun is located on the ridge of a hill at the eastern edge of Ushimado Bay, southeast Okayama Prefecture.[http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/kurashi/kankorekishi/rekishi/jyuyobunkazai/shisekikoukou/shijyuyo/1418099490778.html Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun] (Setouchi City website). Ushimado Bay contains five keyhole-shaped Kofuns: this mound, {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}, {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳}}. Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun is the oldest among them.On-site explanation board. The Ushimado Tenjin Shrine is located on its southern side. No typeal excavations have been conducted. The mound has a primitive keyhole type, with its front section undeveloped and flat, oriented south toward the sea. The rear section is believed to have two tiers, while the front section has one. Surface features include stone cover (rounded cobblestones) and haniwa fragments (cylindrical and jar-shaped). The burial facility is estimated to be a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, with slab-like stones visible at the rear section's summit. burial goods have not been clearly identified. The construction is estimated to date to the mid-to-late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the early Kofun period. Its strategic placement overlooking the sea passage between Ushimado Bay and Maeshima, known as "Karakoto Seto" or "Ushimado Seto," emphasizes the region's political importance during its construction. The site is protected as a Setouchi City other_designation. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 85 meters * Rear section: ''' Diameter: Approximately 50 meters ''' Height: Approximately 7-8 meters * Front section: ''' Length: Approximately 35 meters ''' Width: Approximately 25-30 meters ''' Height: Approximately 4 meters The 80-meter-class size identifies it as belonging to the highest-ranking leaders of the Yoshii River's eastern coastal region. The stone slabs presumed to be part of the burial chamber were sourced from {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島}} in Kagawa Prefecture. It is other_designation1 as a Setouchi City other_designation under the name "Tenjinyama Kofun." == Overview == This Kofun is located on the ridge of a hill at the eastern edge of Ushimado Bay, southeast Okayama Prefecture.[http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/kurashi/kankorekishi/rekishi/jyuyobunkazai/shisekikoukou/shijyuyo/1418099490778.html Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun] (Setouchi City website). Ushimado Bay contains five keyhole-shaped Kofuns: this mound, {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}, {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳}}. Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun is the oldest among them.On-site explanation board. The Ushimado Tenjin Shrine is located on its southern side. No typeal excavations have been conducted. The mound has a primitive keyhole type, with its front section undeveloped and flat, oriented south toward the sea. The rear section is believed to have two tiers, while the front section has one. Surface features include stone cover (rounded cobblestones) and haniwa fragments (cylindrical and jar-shaped). The burial facility is estimated to be a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, with slab-like stones visible at the rear section's summit. burial goods have not been clearly identified. The construction is estimated to date to the mid-to-late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, during the early Kofun period. Its strategic placement overlooking the sea passage between Ushimado Bay and Maeshima, known as "Karakoto Seto" or "Ushimado Seto," emphasizes the region's political importance during its construction. The site is protected as a Setouchi City other_designation. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 85 meters * Rear section: ''' Diameter: Approximately 50 meters ''' Height: Approximately 7-8 meters * Front section: ''' Length: Approximately 35 meters ''' Width: Approximately 25-30 meters ''' Height: Approximately 4 meters The 80-meter-class size identifies it as belonging to the highest-ranking leaders of the Yoshii River's eastern coastal region. The stone slabs presumed to be part of the burial chamber were sourced from {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島}} in Kagawa Prefecture.
