Height: 8 m (rear section) | burial_facilities = (Estimated) {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} | burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} fragments | satellite_tombs = | built = Mid-to-late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|12=draft}} | Interred_individual = | mausoleum = | historic_site = Designated Setouchi City Historic Site ("Tenjinyama Kofun") | tangible_notes = | remarks = | map = {{location map|Japan Okayama|coordinates=34|coordinates=37|coordinates=0.87|lon_deg=134|lon_min=9|lon_sec=56.77|map_dot_label= Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} }} [[File:Ushimado-bay viewed from Ushimado Olive Garden-3.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|location of the Kofun (lower left-center)}}{{small|Viewed from the {{ill|Ushimado Olive Garden|ja|牛窓オリーブ園|12=draft}}. The far-right island is {{ill|Kuroshima|ja|黒島|12=draft}}, where {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|12=draft}} is located. To the left of Kuroshima is {{ill|Maeshima (Okayama)|ja|前島|12=draft}}, with {{ill|Shodoshima|ja|小豆島|12=draft}} visible in the far background. The sea between the mound and Maeshima is known as Karakoto Seto or Ushimado Seto.}]]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300| height =250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1=Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1=bottom | map_dot_label-color1=black | mark1=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.svg | mark-size1=25 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2=Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2=top | map_dot_label-color2=black | mark2=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.svg | mark-size2=25 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3=Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3=bottom | map_dot_label-color3=black | mark3=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.svg | mark-size3=25 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4=(Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4=bottom | map_dot_label-color4=black | mark4=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.svg | mark-size4=25 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5=Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5=right | map_dot_label-color5=black | mark5=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.svg | mark-size5=25 }} '''Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun''' (牛窓天神山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|12=draft}}. It is Historic Site1 as a Setouchi City Historic Site under the name "Tenjinyama Kofun." == Overview == This Kofun is located on the ridge of a hill at the eastern edge of Ushimado Bay, southeast Okayama Prefecture.[http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/kurashi/kankorekishi/rekishi/jyuyobunkazai/shisekikoukou/shijyuyo/1418099490778.html Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun] (Setouchi City website). Ushimado Bay contains five keyhole-shaped Kofuns: this mound, {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|12=draft}}. Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun is the oldest among them.On-site explanation board. The Ushimado Tenjin Shrine is located on its southern side. No typeal excavations have been conducted. The mound has a primitive keyhole type, with its front section undeveloped and flat, oriented south toward the sea. The rear section is believed to have two tiers, while the front section has one. Surface features include stone cover (rounded cobblestones) and haniwa fragments (cylindrical and jar-shaped). The burial facility is estimated to be a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, with slab-like stones visible at the rear section's summit. burial goods have not been clearly identified. The construction is estimated to date to the mid-to-late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|12=draft}}, during the early Kofun period. Its strategic placement overlooking the sea passage between Ushimado Bay and Maeshima, known as "Karakoto Seto" or "Ushimado Seto," emphasizes the region's political importance during its construction. The site is protected as a Setouchi City Historic Site. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 85 meters * Rear section: ''' Diameter: Approximately 50 meters ''' Height: Approximately 7-8 meters * Front section: ''' Length: Approximately 35 meters ''' Width: Approximately 25-30 meters ''' Height: Approximately 4 meters The 80-meter-class size identifies it as belonging to the highest-ranking leaders of the Yoshii River's eastern coastal region. The stone slabs presumed to be part of the burial chamber were sourced from {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島|12=draft}} in Kagawa Prefecture. File:Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun, funcho.jpg|Summit of the rear section File:Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View of the rear section from the front File:Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View of the front section from the rear File:Ushimado Tenjinja, haiden.jpg|Ushimado Tenjin Shrine, located on the southern side == Historic Site == === Setouchi City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' Tenjinyama Kofun{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/91/bunkazai_itiran(20161001).pdf Designated Historic Site List]}} (Setouchi City website). == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not used in this article)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=Ushimado Tenjinyama Height: 8 m (rear section) | burial_facilities = (Estimated) {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} | burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} fragments | satellite_tombs = | built = Mid-to-late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|12=draft}} | Interred_individual = | mausoleum = | historic_site = Designated Setouchi City Historic Site ("Tenjinyama Kofun") | tangible_notes = | remarks = | map = {{location map|Japan Okayama|coordinates=34|coordinates=37|coordinates=0.87|lon_deg=134|lon_min=9|lon_sec=56.77|map_dot_label= Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} }} [[File:Ushimado-bay viewed from Ushimado Olive Garden-3.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|location of the Kofun (lower left-center)}}{{small|Viewed from the {{ill|Ushimado Olive Garden|ja|牛窓オリーブ園|12=draft}}. The far-right island is {{ill|Kuroshima|ja|黒島|12=draft}}, where {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|12=draft}} is located. To the left of Kuroshima is {{ill|Maeshima (Okayama)|ja|前島|12=draft}}, with {{ill|Shodoshima|ja|小豆島|12=draft}} visible in the far background. The sea between the mound and Maeshima is known as Karakoto Seto or Ushimado Seto.}]]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300| height =250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1=Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1=bottom | map_dot_label-color1=black | mark1=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.svg | mark-size1=25 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2=Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2=top | map_dot_label-color2=black | mark2=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.svg | mark-size2=25 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3=Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3=bottom | map_dot_label-color3=black | mark3=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.svg | mark-size3=25 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4=(Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4=bottom | map_dot_label-color4=black | mark4=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.svg | mark-size4=25 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5=Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5=right | map_dot_label-color5=black | mark5=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.svg | mark-size5=25 }} '''Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun''' (牛窓天神山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|12=draft}}. It is Historic Site1 as a Setouchi City Historic Site under the name "Tenjinyama Kofun." == Overview == This Kofun is located on the ridge of a hill at the eastern edge of Ushimado Bay, southeast Okayama Prefecture.[http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/kurashi/kankorekishi/rekishi/jyuyobunkazai/shisekikoukou/shijyuyo/1418099490778.html Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun] (Setouchi City website). Ushimado Bay contains five keyhole-shaped Kofuns: this mound, {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|12=draft}}. Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun is the oldest among them.On-site explanation board. The Ushimado Tenjin Shrine is located on its southern side. No typeal excavations have been conducted. The mound has a primitive keyhole type, with its front section undeveloped and flat, oriented south toward the sea. The rear section is believed to have two tiers, while the front section has one. Surface features include stone cover (rounded cobblestones) and haniwa fragments (cylindrical and jar-shaped). The burial facility is estimated to be a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, with slab-like stones visible at the rear section's summit. burial goods have not been clearly identified. The construction is estimated to date to the mid-to-late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|12=draft}}, during the early Kofun period. Its strategic placement overlooking the sea passage between Ushimado Bay and Maeshima, known as "Karakoto Seto" or "Ushimado Seto," emphasizes the region's political importance during its construction. The site is protected as a Setouchi City Historic Site. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 85 meters * Rear section: ''' Diameter: Approximately 50 meters ''' Height: Approximately 7-8 meters * Front section: ''' Length: Approximately 35 meters ''' Width: Approximately 25-30 meters ''' Height: Approximately 4 meters The 80-meter-class size identifies it as belonging to the highest-ranking leaders of the Yoshii River's eastern coastal region. The stone slabs presumed to be part of the burial chamber were sourced from {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島|12=draft}} in Kagawa Prefecture. File:Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun, funcho.jpg|Summit of the rear section File:Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View of the rear section from the front File:Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View of the front section from the rear File:Ushimado Tenjinja, haiden.jpg|Ushimado Tenjin Shrine, located on the southern side == Historic Site == === Setouchi City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' Tenjinyama Kofun{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/91/bunkazai_itiran(20161001).pdf Designated Historic Site List]}} (Setouchi City website). == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not used in this article)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=Ushimado Tenjinyama The sea between the mound and Maeshima is known as Karakoto Seto or Ushimado Seto.}]]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300| height =250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1=Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1=bottom | map_dot_label-color1=black | mark1=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.svg | mark-size1=25 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2=Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2=top | map_dot_label-color2=black | mark2=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.svg | mark-size2=25 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3=Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3=bottom | map_dot_label-color3=black | mark3=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.svg | mark-size3=25 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4=(Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4=bottom | map_dot_label-color4=black | mark4=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.svg | mark-size4=25 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5=Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5=right | map_dot_label-color5=black | mark5=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.svg | mark-size5=25 }} '''Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun''' (牛窓天神山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is Historic Site1 as a Setouchi City Historic Site under the name "Tenjinyama Kofun." == Overview == This Kofun is located on the ridge of a hill at the eastern edge of Ushimado Bay, southeast Okayama Prefecture.[http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/kurashi/kankorekishi/rekishi/jyuyobunkazai/shisekikoukou/shijyuyo/1418099490778.html Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun] (Setouchi City website). Ushimado Bay contains five keyhole-shaped Kofuns: this mound, {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}. Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun is the oldest among them.On-site explanation board. The Ushimado Tenjin Shrine is located on its southern side. No typeal excavations have been conducted. The mound has a primitive keyhole type, with its front section undeveloped and flat, oriented south toward the sea. The rear section is believed to have two tiers, while the front section has one. Surface features include stone cover (rounded cobblestones) and haniwa fragments (cylindrical and jar-shaped). The burial facility is estimated to be a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}, with slab-like stones visible at the rear section's summit. burial goods have not been clearly identified. The construction is estimated to date to the mid-to-late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, during the early Kofun period. Its strategic placement overlooking the sea passage between Ushimado Bay and Maeshima, known as "Karakoto Seto" or "Ushimado Seto," emphasizes the region's political importance during its construction. The site is protected as a Setouchi City Historic Site. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 85 meters * Rear section: ''' Diameter: Approximately 50 meters ''' Height: Approximately 7-8 meters * Front section: ''' Length: Approximately 35 meters ''' Width: Approximately 25-30 meters ''' Height: Approximately 4 meters The 80-meter-class size identifies it as belonging to the highest-ranking leaders of the Yoshii River's eastern coastal region. The stone slabs presumed to be part of the burial chamber were sourced from {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島|12=draft|12=draft}} in Kagawa Prefecture. File:Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun, funcho.jpg|Summit of the rear section File:Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View of the rear section from the front File:Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View of the front section from the rear File:Ushimado Tenjinja, haiden.jpg|Ushimado Tenjin Shrine, located on the southern side == Historic Site == === Setouchi City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' Tenjinyama Kofun{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/91/bunkazai_itiran(20161001).pdf Designated Historic Site List]}} (Setouchi City website). == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not used in this article)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|title=Okayama Prefecture Archaeological Materials Vol. 18|publisher=Okayama Prefecture|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/kurashi/kankorekishi/rekishi/jyuyobunkazai/shisekikoukou/shijyuyo/1418099490778.html Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun] - Setouchi City Website {{citation needed|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun}} {{citation needed|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun}} {{citation needed|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun}} {{citation needed|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun}} {{citation needed|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun}} {{citation needed|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:City-Historic Site1 historic sites in Okayama]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun]] [[en:Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun]] [[ja:牛窓天神山古墳]] [[sn:shimado Tenjinyama Kofun]]

The site is protected as a Setouchi City Historic Site. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 85 meters * Rear section: ''' Diameter: Approximately 50 meters ''' Height: Approximately 7-8 meters * Front section: ''' Length: Approximately 35 meters ''' Width: Approximately 25-30 meters ''' Height: Approximately 4 meters The 80-meter-class size identifies it as belonging to the highest-ranking leaders of the Yoshii River's eastern coastal region. The stone slabs presumed to be part of the burial chamber were sourced from {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島|12=draft|12=draft}} in Kagawa Prefecture. File:Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun, funcho.jpg|Summit of the rear section File:Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View of the rear section from the front File:Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View of the front section from the rear File:Ushimado Tenjinja, haiden.jpg|Ushimado Tenjin Shrine, located on the southern side == Historic Site == === Setouchi City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' Tenjinyama Kofun{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/91/bunkazai_itiran(20161001).pdf Designated Historic Site List]}} (Setouchi City website). == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not used in this article)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|title=Okayama Prefecture Archaeological Materials Vol. 18|publisher=Okayama Prefecture|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/kurashi/kankorekishi/rekishi/jyuyobunkazai/shisekikoukou/shijyuyo/1418099490778.html Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun] - {{afc comment|1=See [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun | image = [[File:Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
View of the mound (left: front section, back-right: rear section) | alternate_name = | affiliation = | location = {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|12=draft|12=draft}} | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 37 | coordinates = 0.87 | coordinates = 134 | coordinates = 9 | coordinates = 56.77 | iso = JP-33 | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} | area = Length: 85 m
Height: 8 m (rear section) | burial_facilities = (Estimated) {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} | burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} fragments | satellite_tombs = | built = Mid-to-late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|12=draft|12=draft}} | Interred_individual = | mausoleum = | historic_site = Designated Setouchi City Historic Site ("Tenjinyama Kofun") | tangible_notes = | remarks = | map = {{location map|Japan Okayama|coordinates=34|coordinates=37|coordinates=0.87|lon_deg=134|lon_min=9|lon_sec=56.77|map_dot_label= Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} }} [[File:Ushimado-bay viewed from Ushimado Olive Garden-3.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|location of the Kofun (lower left-center)}}{{small|Viewed from the {{ill|Ushimado Olive Garden|ja|牛窓オリーブ園|12=draft|12=draft}}. The far-right island is {{ill|Kuroshima|ja|黒島|12=draft|12=draft}}, where {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} is located. To the left of Kuroshima is {{ill|Maeshima (Okayama)|ja|前島|12=draft|12=draft}}, with {{ill|Shodoshima|ja|小豆島|12=draft|12=draft}} visible in the far background. The sea between the mound and Maeshima is known as Karakoto Seto or Ushimado Seto.}]]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300| height =250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1=Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1=bottom | map_dot_label-color1=black | mark1=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.svg | mark-size1=25 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2=Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2=top | map_dot_label-color2=black | mark2=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.svg | mark-size2=25 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3=Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3=bottom | map_dot_label-color3=black | mark3=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.svg | mark-size3=25 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4=(Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4=bottom | map_dot_label-color4=black | mark4=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.svg | mark-size4=25 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5=Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5=right | map_dot_label-color5=black | mark5=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.svg | mark-size5=25 }} '''Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun''' (牛窓天神山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is Historic Site1 as a Setouchi City Historic Site under the name "Tenjinyama Kofun." == Overview == This Kofun is located on the ridge of a hill at the eastern edge of Ushimado Bay, southeast Okayama Prefecture.[http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/kurashi/kankorekishi/rekishi/jyuyobunkazai/shisekikoukou/shijyuyo/1418099490778.html Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun] (Setouchi City website). Ushimado Bay contains five keyhole-shaped Kofuns: this mound, {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}. Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun is the oldest among them.On-site explanation board. The Ushimado Tenjin Shrine is located on its southern side. No typeal excavations have been conducted. The mound has a primitive keyhole type, with its front section undeveloped and flat, oriented south toward the sea. The rear section is believed to have two tiers, while the front section has one. Surface features include stone cover (rounded cobblestones) and haniwa fragments (cylindrical and jar-shaped). The burial facility is estimated to be a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}, with slab-like stones visible at the rear section's summit. burial goods have not been clearly identified. The construction is estimated to date to the mid-to-late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, during the early Kofun period. Its strategic placement overlooking the sea passage between Ushimado Bay and Maeshima, known as "Karakoto Seto" or "Ushimado Seto," emphasizes the region's political importance during its construction. The site is protected as a Setouchi City Historic Site. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 85 meters * Rear section: ''' Diameter: Approximately 50 meters ''' Height: Approximately 7-8 meters * Front section: ''' Length: Approximately 35 meters ''' Width: Approximately 25-30 meters ''' Height: Approximately 4 meters The 80-meter-class size identifies it as belonging to the highest-ranking leaders of the Yoshii River's eastern coastal region. The stone slabs presumed to be part of the burial chamber were sourced from {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島|12=draft|12=draft}} in Kagawa Prefecture. File:Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun, funcho.jpg|Summit of the rear section File:Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View of the rear section from the front File:Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View of the front section from the rear File:Ushimado Tenjinja, haiden.jpg|Ushimado Tenjin Shrine, located on the southern side == Historic Site == === Setouchi City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' Tenjinyama Kofun{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/91/bunkazai_itiran(20161001).pdf Designated Historic Site List]}} (Setouchi City website). == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not used in this article)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|title=Okayama Prefecture Archaeological Materials Vol. 18|publisher=Okayama Prefecture|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/kurashi/kankorekishi/rekishi/jyuyobunkazai/shisekikoukou/shijyuyo/1418099490778.html Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun] - To the left of Kuroshima is {{ill|Maeshima (Okayama)|ja|前島|12=draft|12=draft}}, with {{ill|Shodoshima|ja|小豆島|12=draft|12=draft}} visible in the far background. The sea between the mound and Maeshima is known as Karakoto Seto or Ushimado Seto.}]]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300| height =250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1=Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1=bottom | map_dot_label-color1=black | mark1=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.svg | mark-size1=25 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2=Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2=top | map_dot_label-color2=black | mark2=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.svg | mark-size2=25 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3=Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3=bottom | map_dot_label-color3=black | mark3=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.svg | mark-size3=25 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4=(Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4=bottom | map_dot_label-color4=black | mark4=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.svg | mark-size4=25 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5=Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5=right | map_dot_label-color5=black | mark5=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.svg | mark-size5=25 }} '''Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun''' (牛窓天神山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is Historic Site1 as a Setouchi City Historic Site under the name "Tenjinyama Kofun." == Overview == This Kofun is located on the ridge of a hill at the eastern edge of Ushimado Bay, southeast Okayama Prefecture.[http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/kurashi/kankorekishi/rekishi/jyuyobunkazai/shisekikoukou/shijyuyo/1418099490778.html Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun] (Setouchi City website). Ushimado Bay contains five keyhole-shaped Kofuns: this mound, {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}. Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun is the oldest among them.On-site explanation board. The Ushimado Tenjin Shrine is located on its southern side. No typeal excavations have been conducted. The mound has a primitive keyhole type, with its front section undeveloped and flat, oriented south toward the sea. The rear section is believed to have two tiers, while the front section has one. Surface features include stone cover (rounded cobblestones) and haniwa fragments (cylindrical and jar-shaped). The burial facility is estimated to be a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}, with slab-like stones visible at the rear section's summit. burial goods have not been clearly identified. The construction is estimated to date to the mid-to-late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, during the early Kofun period. Its strategic placement overlooking the sea passage between Ushimado Bay and Maeshima, known as "Karakoto Seto" or "Ushimado Seto," emphasizes the region's political importance during its construction. The site is protected as a Setouchi City Historic Site. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 85 meters * Rear section: ''' Diameter: Approximately 50 meters ''' Height: Approximately 7-8 meters * Front section: ''' Length: Approximately 35 meters ''' Width: Approximately 25-30 meters ''' Height: Approximately 4 meters The 80-meter-class size identifies it as belonging to the highest-ranking leaders of the Yoshii River's eastern coastal region. The stone slabs presumed to be part of the burial chamber were sourced from {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島|12=draft|12=draft}} in Kagawa Prefecture. The sea between the mound and Maeshima is known as Karakoto Seto or Ushimado Seto.}]]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300| height =250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1=Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1=bottom | map_dot_label-color1=black | mark1=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.svg | mark-size1=25 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2=Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2=top | map_dot_label-color2=black | mark2=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.svg | mark-size2=25 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3=Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3=bottom | map_dot_label-color3=black | mark3=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.svg | mark-size3=25 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4=(Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4=bottom | map_dot_label-color4=black | mark4=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.svg | mark-size4=25 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5=Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5=right | map_dot_label-color5=black | mark5=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.svg | mark-size5=25 }} '''Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun''' (牛窓天神山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is Historic Site1 as a Setouchi City Historic Site under the name "Tenjinyama Kofun." == Overview == This Kofun is located on the ridge of a hill at the eastern edge of Ushimado Bay, southeast Okayama Prefecture.[http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/kurashi/kankorekishi/rekishi/jyuyobunkazai/shisekikoukou/shijyuyo/1418099490778.html Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun] (Setouchi City website). Ushimado Bay contains five keyhole-shaped Kofuns: this mound, {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}. Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun is the oldest among them.On-site explanation board. The Ushimado Tenjin Shrine is located on its southern side. No typeal excavations have been conducted. The mound has a primitive keyhole type, with its front section undeveloped and flat, oriented south toward the sea. The rear section is believed to have two tiers, while the front section has one. Surface features include stone cover (rounded cobblestones) and haniwa fragments (cylindrical and jar-shaped). The burial facility is estimated to be a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}, with slab-like stones visible at the rear section's summit. burial goods have not been clearly identified. The construction is estimated to date to the mid-to-late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, during the early Kofun period. Its strategic placement overlooking the sea passage between Ushimado Bay and Maeshima, known as "Karakoto Seto" or "Ushimado Seto," emphasizes the region's political importance during its construction. The site is protected as a Setouchi City Historic Site. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 85 meters * Rear section: ''' Diameter: Approximately 50 meters ''' Height: Approximately 7-8 meters * Front section: ''' Length: Approximately 35 meters ''' Width: Approximately 25-30 meters ''' Height: Approximately 4 meters The 80-meter-class size identifies it as belonging to the highest-ranking leaders of the Yoshii River's eastern coastal region. The stone slabs presumed to be part of the burial chamber were sourced from {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島|12=draft|12=draft}} in Kagawa Prefecture. Surface features include stone cover (rounded cobblestones) and haniwa fragments (cylindrical and jar-shaped). The burial facility is estimated to be a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}, with slab-like stones visible at the rear section's summit. burial goods have not been clearly identified. The construction is estimated to date to the mid-to-late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, during the early Kofun period. Its strategic placement overlooking the sea passage between Ushimado Bay and Maeshima, known as "Karakoto Seto" or "Ushimado Seto," emphasizes the region's political importance during its construction. The site is protected as a Setouchi City Historic Site. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 85 meters * Rear section: ''' Diameter: Approximately 50 meters ''' Height: Approximately 7-8 meters * Front section: ''' Length: Approximately 35 meters ''' Width: Approximately 25-30 meters ''' Height: Approximately 4 meters The 80-meter-class size identifies it as belonging to the highest-Ranking leaders of the Yoshii River's eastern coastal region. The stone slabs presumed to be part of the burial chamber were sourced from {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島|12=draft|12=draft}} in Kagawa Prefecture. File:Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun, funcho.jpg|Summit of the rear section File:Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View of the rear section from the front File:Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View of the front section from the rear File:Ushimado Tenjinja, haiden.jpg|Ushimado Tenjin Shrine, located on the southern side == Historic Site == === Setouchi City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' Tenjinyama Kofun{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/91/bunkazai_itiran(20161001).pdf Designated Historic Site List]}} (Setouchi City website). == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not used in this article)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|title=Okayama Prefecture Archaeological Materials Vol. 18|publisher=Okayama Prefecture|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/kurashi/kankorekishi/rekishi/jyuyobunkazai/shisekikoukou/shijyuyo/1418099490778.html Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun] - So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun | image = [[File:Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
View of the mound (left: front section, back-right: rear section) | alternate_name = | affiliation = | location = {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|12=draft|12=draft}} | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 37 | coordinates = 0.87 | coordinates = 134 | coordinates = 9 | coordinates = 56.77 | iso = JP-33 | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} | area = Length: 85 m
Height: 8 m (rear section) | burial_facilities = (Estimated) {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} | burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} fragments | satellite_tombs = | built = Mid-to-late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|12=draft|12=draft}} | Interred_individual = | mausoleum = | historic_site = Designated Setouchi City Historic Site ("Tenjinyama Kofun") | tangible_notes = | remarks = | map = {{location map|Japan Okayama|coordinates=34|coordinates=37|coordinates=0.87|lon_deg=134|lon_min=9|lon_sec=56.77|map_dot_label= Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} }} [[File:Ushimado-bay viewed from Ushimado Olive Garden-3.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|location of the Kofun (lower left-center)}}{{small|Viewed from the {{ill|Ushimado Olive Garden|ja|牛窓オリーブ園|12=draft|12=draft}}. The far-right island is {{ill|Kuroshima|ja|黒島|12=draft|12=draft}}, where {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} is located. To the left of Kuroshima is {{ill|Maeshima (Okayama)|ja|前島|12=draft|12=draft}}, with {{ill|Shodoshima|ja|小豆島|12=draft|12=draft}} visible in the far background. The sea between the mound and Maeshima is known as Karakoto Seto or Ushimado Seto.}]]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300| height =250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1=Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1=bottom | map_dot_label-color1=black | mark1=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.svg | mark-size1=25 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2=Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2=top | map_dot_label-color2=black | mark2=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.svg | mark-size2=25 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3=Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3=bottom | map_dot_label-color3=black | mark3=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.svg | mark-size3=25 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4=(Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4=bottom | map_dot_label-color4=black | mark4=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.svg | mark-size4=25 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5=Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5=right | map_dot_label-color5=black | mark5=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.svg | mark-size5=25 }} '''Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun''' (牛窓天神山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is Historic Site1 as a Setouchi City Historic Site under the name "Tenjinyama Kofun." == Overview == This Kofun is located on the ridge of a hill at the eastern edge of Ushimado Bay, southeast Okayama Prefecture.[http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/kurashi/kankorekishi/rekishi/jyuyobunkazai/shisekikoukou/shijyuyo/1418099490778.html Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun] (Setouchi City website). Ushimado Bay contains five keyhole-shaped Kofuns: this mound, {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}. Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun is the oldest among them.On-site explanation board. The Ushimado Tenjin Shrine is located on its southern side. No typeal excavations have been conducted. The mound has a primitive keyhole type, with its front section undeveloped and flat, oriented south toward the sea. The rear section is believed to have two tiers, while the front section has one. Surface features include stone cover (rounded cobblestones) and haniwa fragments (cylindrical and jar-shaped). The burial facility is estimated to be a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}, with slab-like stones visible at the rear section's summit. burial goods have not been clearly identified. The construction is estimated to date to the mid-to-late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, during the early Kofun period. Its strategic placement overlooking the sea passage between Ushimado Bay and Maeshima, known as "Karakoto Seto" or "Ushimado Seto," emphasizes the region's political importance during its construction. The site is protected as a Setouchi City Historic Site. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 85 meters * Rear section: ''' Diameter: Approximately 50 meters ''' Height: Approximately 7-8 meters * Front section: ''' Length: Approximately 35 meters ''' Width: Approximately 25-30 meters ''' Height: Approximately 4 meters The 80-meter-class size identifies it as belonging to the highest-Ranking leaders of the Yoshii River's eastern coastal region. The stone slabs presumed to be part of the burial chamber were sourced from {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島|12=draft|12=draft}} in Kagawa Prefecture. File:Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun, funcho.jpg|Summit of the rear section File:Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View of the rear section from the front File:Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View of the front section from the rear File:Ushimado Tenjinja, haiden.jpg|Ushimado Tenjin Shrine, located on the southern side == Historic Site == === Setouchi City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' Tenjinyama Kofun{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/91/bunkazai_itiran(20161001).pdf Designated Historic Site List]}} (Setouchi City website). == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not used in this article)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|title=Okayama Prefecture Archaeological Materials Vol. 18|publisher=Okayama Prefecture|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/kurashi/kankorekishi/rekishi/jyuyobunkazai/shisekikoukou/shijyuyo/1418099490778.html Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun] - The stone slabs presumed to be part of the burial chamber were sourced from {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Kagawa Prefecture. Setouchi City Website {{citation needed|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun}} {{citation needed|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun}} {{citation needed|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun}} {{citation needed|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun}} {{citation needed|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun}} {{citation needed|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:City-Historic Site1 historic sites in Okayama]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun]] [[en:Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun]] [[ja:牛窓天神山古墳]] [[sn:shimado Tenjinyama Kofun]]

revision.comment: 11 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:牛窓天神山古墳 up to rev 96036079
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 牛窓天神山古墳 |画像 = [[File:Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（左に前方部、右奥に後円部） |別名 = |所属 = |所在地 =[[岡山県]][[瀬戸内市]][[牛窓|牛窓町牛窓]] |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 37|緯度秒 = 0.87 |経度度 = 134|経度分 = 9|経度秒 = 56.77 |ISO = JP-33 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長85m
高さ8m（後円部） |埋葬施設 = （推定）[[竪穴式石室]] |出土品 = [[埴輪]]片 |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[4世紀]]中葉-後半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 瀬戸内市指定史跡「天神山古墳」 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図2= {{Location map|Japan Okayama|lat_deg=34|lat_min=37|lat_sec=0.87|lon_deg=134|lon_min=9|lon_sec=56.77|label= 牛窓天神山古墳|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} }} [[File:Ushimado-bay viewed from Ushimado Olive Garden-3.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|古墳の所在する丘陵（中央左下）}}{{small|[[牛窓オリーブ園]]から望む。画像右端の黒島に[[黒島古墳|黒島1号墳]]が所在する。黒島の左は[[前島 (岡山県)|前島]]、最奥左は[[小豆島]]で、牛窓天神山古墳の丘陵と前島との間に唐琴の瀬戸（牛窓瀬戸）が流れる。}}]] {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300| height =250 | caption = {{center|牛窓湾の前方後円墳分布図}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | label1=牛窓天神山 | label-pos1=bottom | label-color1=black | mark1=前方後円墳-南南東.svg | mark-size1=25 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | label2=黒島 | label-pos2=top | label-color2=black | mark2=前方後円墳-南西.svg | mark-size2=25 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | label3=鹿歩山 | label-pos3=bottom | label-color3=black | mark3=前方後円墳-北東.svg | mark-size3=25 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | label4=(波歌山) | label-pos4=bottom | label-color4=black | mark4=前方後円墳-南西.svg | mark-size4=25 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | label5=二塚山 | label-pos5=right | label-color5=black | mark5=前方後円墳-南東.svg | mark-size5=25 }} '''牛窓天神山古墳'''（うしまどてんじんやまこふん）は、[[岡山県]][[瀬戸内市]][[牛窓|牛窓町牛窓]]にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。瀬戸内市指定史跡に指定されている（指定名称は「天神山古墳」）。 == 概要 ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+牛窓湾の前方後円墳5基 !湾内位置!!古墳名!!墳丘長!!築造時期!!史跡指定 |- |北東||'''牛窓天神山古墳'''||85m||4c中葉-後半||瀬戸内市指定史跡 |- |南||[[黒島古墳|黒島1号墳]]||81m||5c前半-中葉||なし |- |北西||[[鹿歩山古墳]]||84m||5c後半||岡山県指定史跡 |- |北||[[波歌山古墳]]||60m||6c前半||（消滅） |- |南西||[[二塚山古墳 (瀬戸内市)|二塚山古墳]]||55m||6c後半||岡山県指定史跡 |}
岡山県南東部、牛窓湾東端の丘陵の山頂尾根上に築造された古墳である[http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/kurashi/kankorekishi/rekishi/jyuyobunkazai/shisekikoukou/shijyuyo/1418099490778.html 牛窓天神山古墳]（瀬戸内市ホームページ）。 。牛窓湾では、本古墳のほか[[二塚山古墳 (瀬戸内市)|二塚山古墳]]・[[鹿歩山古墳]]・[[波歌山古墳]]（消滅）・[[黒島古墳|黒島1号墳]]の前方後円墳5基が湾を取り囲むように営造されたことが知られる。現在では古墳南側に牛窓天神社が鎮座する史跡説明板。。これまでに発掘調査は実施されていない{{Sfn|牛窓町史 資料編II|1997|pp=132-137}}。 墳形は前方部が未発達で低平な前方後円形で、前方部を南方向（海側）に向ける{{Sfn|牛窓町史 資料編II|1997|pp=132-137}}{{Sfn|天神山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}。墳丘は後円部で2段築成、前方部で1段築成と見られる{{Sfn|牛窓町史 資料編II|1997|pp=132-137}}。墳丘表面では[[葺石]]（円礫）、[[埴輪]]（[[円筒埴輪]]・壺形埴輪）が認められている{{Sfn|天神山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}{{Sfn|牛窓町史 資料編II|1997|pp=132-137}}。埋葬施設は明らかでないが、[[竪穴式石室]]であったと推定され{{Sfn|牛窓町古墳図|2002|p=70}}、後円部墳頂において板状割石が露出する{{Sfn|牛窓町史 資料編II|1997|pp=132-137}}。出土遺物は明らかでない{{Sfn|天神山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]前期の[[4世紀]]中葉-後半頃と推定される{{Sfn|牛窓町史 資料編II|1997|pp=132-137}}。牛窓湾の前方後円墳5基のうちでは最初の築造に位置づけられ{{Sfn|天神山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}、他の前方後円墳4基とともに当時の牛窓湾の港湾としての政治的重要性を示す古墳になる{{Sfn|天神山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}。特に本古墳眼下の前島との間の海は「唐琴の瀬戸」・「牛窓瀬戸」と称される航路であるため、牛窓最古の前方後円墳としての築造背景が示唆される{{Sfn|牛窓町史 資料編II|1997|pp=132-137}}。 古墳域は瀬戸内市指定史跡に指定されている。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|牛窓町史 資料編II|1997|pp=132-137}}。 * 墳丘長：約85メートル * 後円部 - 2段築成。 ** 直径：約50メートル ** 高さ：約7-8メートル * 前方部 - 1段築成か。 ** 長さ：約35メートル ** 幅：約25-30メートル ** 高さ：約4メートル 墳丘長80メートル代という規模は、吉井川東岸南部地域では最高首長層に位置づけられる{{Sfn|牛窓町史 通史編|2001|pp=121-123}}。また、竪穴式石室の石材と推定される後円部墳頂の板状割石は[[香川県]]の[[屋島]]産とされ、一宮天神山古墳と共通する{{Sfn|牛窓町史 通史編|2001|pp=121-123}}。ただし[[浦間茶臼山古墳]]・[[網浜茶臼山古墳]]など吉備の主な前期古墳は香川県の[[豊島]]産の石材を使用しており、それら産出地の相違が注意される{{Sfn|牛窓町史 通史編|2001|pp=121-123}}。 File:Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun, funcho.jpg|後円部墳頂 File:Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|前方部から後円部を望む File:Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|後円部から前方部を望む File:Ushimado Tenjinja, haiden.jpg|南側に鎮座する牛窓天神社 == 文化財 == === 瀬戸内市指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 天神山古墳{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/91/bunkazai_itiran(20161001).pdf 指定文化財・登録文化財一覧]}}（瀬戸内市ホームページ）。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（瀬戸内市教育委員会設置） * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1997|chapter=牛窓天神山古墳|title=牛窓町史 資料編II（考古・古代・中世・近世）|publisher=牛窓町|isbn=|pages=132-137|ref={{Harvid|牛窓町史 資料編II|1997}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2001|chapter=|title=牛窓町史 通史編|publisher=牛窓町|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|牛窓町史 通史編|2001}}}} ** {{Cite book |和書 |editor= |author=牛窓町教育委員会 |year=2002 |chapter=牛窓天神山古墳 |url= http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/12639 |title=牛窓町古墳図 |publisher= |isbn= |page=70 |ref={{Harvid|牛窓町古墳図|2002}} }} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=天神山古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 34 岡山県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|天神山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=高橋護|year=1989|chapter=天神山古墳 > 牛窓天神山古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|牛窓天神山古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=牛窓天神山古墳|title=岡山県史 一八 考古資料|publisher=岡山県|isbn=|ref=}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/kurashi/kankorekishi/rekishi/jyuyobunkazai/shisekikoukou/shijyuyo/1418099490778.html 牛窓天神山古墳] - 瀬戸内市ホームページ {{DEFAULTSORT:うしまとてんしんやまこふん}} [[Category:岡山県の古墳]] [[Category:瀬戸内市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:岡山県内の市町村指定史跡]]

高さ8m（後円部） |埋葬施設 = （推定）[[竪穴式石室]] |出土品 = [[埴輪]]片 |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[4世紀]]中葉-後半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 瀬戸内市指定史跡「天神山古墳」 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図2= {{Location map|Japan Okayama|lat_deg=34|lat_min=37|lat_sec=0.87|lon_deg=134|lon_min=9|lon_sec=56.77|label= 牛窓天神山古墳|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} }} [[File:Ushimado-bay viewed from Ushimado Olive Garden-3.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|古墳の所在する丘陵（中央左下）}}{{small|[[牛窓オリーブ園]]から望む。画像右端の黒島に[[黒島古墳|黒島1号墳]]が所在する。黒島の左は[[前島 (岡山県)|前島]]、最奥左は[[小豆島]]で、牛窓天神山古墳の丘陵と前島との間に唐琴の瀬戸（牛窓瀬戸）が流れる。}}]] {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300| height =250 | caption = {{center|牛窓湾の前方後円墳分布図}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | label1=牛窓天神山 | label-pos1=bottom | label-color1=black | mark1=前方後円墳-南南東.svg | mark-size1=25 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | label2=黒島 | label-pos2=top | label-color2=black | mark2=前方後円墳-南西.svg | mark-size2=25 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | label3=鹿歩山 | label-pos3=bottom | label-color3=black | mark3=前方後円墳-北東.svg | mark-size3=25 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | label4=(波歌山) | label-pos4=bottom | label-color4=black | mark4=前方後円墳-南西.svg | mark-size4=25 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | label5=二塚山 | label-pos5=right | label-color5=black | mark5=前方後円墳-南東.svg | mark-size5=25 }} '''牛窓天神山古墳'''（うしまどてんじんやまこふん）は、[[岡山県]][[瀬戸内市]][[牛窓|牛窓町牛窓]]にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。瀬戸内市指定史跡に指定されている（指定名称は「天神山古墳」）。 == 概要 ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+牛窓湾の前方後円墳5基 !湾内位置!!古墳名!!墳丘長!!築造時期!!史跡指定 |- |北東||'''牛窓天神山古墳'''||85m||4c中葉-後半||瀬戸内市指定史跡 |- |南||[[黒島古墳|黒島1号墳]]||81m||5c前半-中葉||なし |- |北西||[[鹿歩山古墳]]||84m||5c後半||岡山県指定史跡 |- |北||[[波歌山古墳]]||60m||6c前半||（消滅） |- |南西||[[二塚山古墳 (瀬戸内市)|二塚山古墳]]||55m||6c後半||岡山県指定史跡 |}
岡山県南東部、牛窓湾東端の丘陵の山頂尾根上に築造された古墳である[http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/kurashi/kankorekishi/rekishi/jyuyobunkazai/shisekikoukou/shijyuyo/1418099490778.html 牛窓天神山古墳]（瀬戸内市ホームページ）。 。牛窓湾では、本古墳のほか[[二塚山古墳 (瀬戸内市)|二塚山古墳]]・[[鹿歩山古墳]]・[[波歌山古墳]]（消滅）・[[黒島古墳|黒島1号墳]]の前方後円墳5基が湾を取り囲むように営造されたことが知られる。現在では古墳南側に牛窓天神社が鎮座する史跡説明板。。これまでに発掘調査は実施されていない{{Sfn|牛窓町史 資料編II|1997|pp=132-137}}。 墳形は前方部が未発達で低平な前方後円形で、前方部を南方向（海側）に向ける{{Sfn|牛窓町史 資料編II|1997|pp=132-137}}{{Sfn|天神山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}。墳丘は後円部で2段築成、前方部で1段築成と見られる{{Sfn|牛窓町史 資料編II|1997|pp=132-137}}。墳丘表面では[[葺石]]（円礫）、[[埴輪]]（[[円筒埴輪]]・壺形埴輪）が認められている{{Sfn|天神山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}{{Sfn|牛窓町史 資料編II|1997|pp=132-137}}。埋葬施設は明らかでないが、[[竪穴式石室]]であったと推定され{{Sfn|牛窓町古墳図|2002|p=70}}、後円部墳頂において板状割石が露出する{{Sfn|牛窓町史 資料編II|1997|pp=132-137}}。出土遺物は明らかでない{{Sfn|天神山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]前期の[[4世紀]]中葉-後半頃と推定される{{Sfn|牛窓町史 資料編II|1997|pp=132-137}}。牛窓湾の前方後円墳5基のうちでは最初の築造に位置づけられ{{Sfn|天神山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}、他の前方後円墳4基とともに当時の牛窓湾の港湾としての政治的重要性を示す古墳になる{{Sfn|天神山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}。特に本古墳眼下の前島との間の海は「唐琴の瀬戸」・「牛窓瀬戸」と称される航路であるため、牛窓最古の前方後円墳としての築造背景が示唆される{{Sfn|牛窓町史 資料編II|1997|pp=132-137}}。 古墳域は瀬戸内市指定史跡に指定されている。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|牛窓町史 資料編II|1997|pp=132-137}}。 * 墳丘長：約85メートル * 後円部 - 2段築成。 ** 直径：約50メートル ** 高さ：約7-8メートル * 前方部 - 1段築成か。 ** 長さ：約35メートル ** 幅：約25-30メートル ** 高さ：約4メートル 墳丘長80メートル代という規模は、吉井川東岸南部地域では最高首長層に位置づけられる{{Sfn|牛窓町史 通史編|2001|pp=121-123}}。また、竪穴式石室の石材と推定される後円部墳頂の板状割石は[[香川県]]の[[屋島]]産とされ、一宮天神山古墳と共通する{{Sfn|牛窓町史 通史編|2001|pp=121-123}}。ただし[[浦間茶臼山古墳]]・[[網浜茶臼山古墳]]など吉備の主な前期古墳は香川県の[[豊島]]産の石材を使用しており、それら産出地の相違が注意される{{Sfn|牛窓町史 通史編|2001|pp=121-123}}。 File:Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun, funcho.jpg|後円部墳頂 File:Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|前方部から後円部を望む File:Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|後円部から前方部を望む File:Ushimado Tenjinja, haiden.jpg|南側に鎮座する牛窓天神社 == 文化財 == === 瀬戸内市指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 天神山古墳{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/91/bunkazai_itiran(20161001).pdf 指定文化財・登録文化財一覧]}}（瀬戸内市ホームページ）。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（瀬戸内市教育委員会設置） * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1997|chapter=牛窓天神山古墳|title=牛窓町史 資料編II（考古・古代・中世・近世）|publisher=牛窓町|isbn=|pages=132-137|ref={{Harvid|牛窓町史 資料編II|1997}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2001|chapter=|title=牛窓町史 通史編|publisher=牛窓町|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|牛窓町史 通史編|2001}}}} ** {{Cite book |和書 |editor= |author=牛窓町教育委員会 |year=2002 |chapter=牛窓天神山古墳 |url= http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/12639 |title=牛窓町古墳図 |publisher= |isbn= |page=70 |ref={{Harvid|牛窓町古墳図|2002}} }} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=天神山古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 34 岡山県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|天神山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=高橋護|year=1989|chapter=天神山古墳 > 牛窓天神山古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|牛窓天神山古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=牛窓天神山古墳|title=岡山県史 一八 考古資料|publisher=岡山県|isbn=|ref=}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/kurashi/kankorekishi/rekishi/jyuyobunkazai/shisekikoukou/shijyuyo/1418099490778.html 牛窓天神山古墳] - The far-right island is {{ill|Kuroshima|ja|黒島|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, where {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is located. To the left of Kuroshima is {{ill|Maeshima (Okayama)|ja|前島|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, with {{ill|Shodoshima|ja|小豆島|qq=draft|qq=draft}} visible in the far background. The sea between the mound and Maeshima is known as Karakoto Seto or Ushimado Seto.}]]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300| height =250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1=Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1=bottom | map_dot_label-color1=black | mark1=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.svg | mark-size1=25 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2=Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2=top | map_dot_label-color2=black | mark2=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.svg | mark-size2=25 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3=Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3=bottom | map_dot_label-color3=black | mark3=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.svg | mark-size3=25 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4=(Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4=bottom | map_dot_label-color4=black | mark4=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.svg | mark-size4=25 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5=Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5=right | map_dot_label-color5=black | mark5=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.svg | mark-size5=25 }} '''Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun''' (牛窓天神山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is Historic Site1 as a Setouchi City Historic Site under the name "Tenjinyama Kofun." == Overview == This Kofun is located on the ridge of a hill at the eastern edge of Ushimado Bay, southeast Okayama Prefecture.[http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/kurashi/kankorekishi/rekishi/jyuyobunkazai/shisekikoukou/shijyuyo/1418099490778.html Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun] (Setouchi City website). Ushimado Bay contains five keyhole-shaped Kofuns: this mound, {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun is the oldest among them.On-site explanation board. The Ushimado Tenjin Shrine is located on its southern side. No typeal excavations have been conducted. The mound has a primitive keyhole type, with its front section undeveloped and flat, oriented south toward the sea. The rear section is believed to have two tiers, while the front section has one. Surface features include stone cover (rounded cobblestones) and haniwa fragments (cylindrical and jar-shaped). The burial facility is estimated to be a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, with slab-like stones visible at the rear section's summit. burial goods have not been clearly identified. The construction is estimated to date to the mid-to-late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, during the early Kofun period. Its strategic placement overlooking the sea passage between Ushimado Bay and Maeshima, known as "Karakoto Seto" or "Ushimado Seto," emphasizes the region's political importance during its construction. The site is protected as a Setouchi City Historic Site. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 85 meters * Rear section: ''' Diameter: Approximately 50 meters ''' Height: Approximately 7-8 meters * Front section: ''' Length: Approximately 35 meters ''' Width: Approximately 25-30 meters ''' Height: Approximately 4 meters The 80-meter-class size identifies it as belonging to the highest-Ranking leaders of the Yoshii River's eastern coastal region. The stone slabs presumed to be part of the burial chamber were sourced from {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Kagawa Prefecture. File:Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun, funcho.jpg|Summit of the rear section File:Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View of the rear section from the front File:Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View of the front section from the rear File:Ushimado Tenjinja, haiden.jpg|Ushimado Tenjin Shrine, located on the southern side == Historic Site == === Setouchi City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' Tenjinyama Kofun{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/91/bunkazai_itiran(20161001).pdf Designated Historic Site List]}} (Setouchi City website). == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not used in this article)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|title=Okayama Prefecture Archaeological Materials Vol. 18|publisher=Okayama Prefecture|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/kurashi/kankorekishi/rekishi/jyuyobunkazai/shisekikoukou/shijyuyo/1418099490778.html Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun] - The far-right island is {{ill|Kuroshima|ja|黒島|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, where {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is located. To the left of Kuroshima is {{ill|Maeshima (Okayama)|ja|前島|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, with {{ill|Shodoshima|ja|小豆島|qq=draft|qq=draft}} visible in the far background. The sea between the mound and Maeshima is known as Karakoto Seto or Ushimado Seto.}]]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300| height =250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1=Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1=bottom | map_dot_label-color1=black | mark1=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.svg | mark-size1=25 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2=Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2=top | map_dot_label-color2=black | mark2=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.svg | mark-size2=25 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3=Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3=bottom | map_dot_label-color3=black | mark3=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.svg | mark-size3=25 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4=(Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4=bottom | map_dot_label-color4=black | mark4=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.svg | mark-size4=25 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5=Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5=right | map_dot_label-color5=black | mark5=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.svg | mark-size5=25 }} '''Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun''' (牛窓天神山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is Historic Site1 as a Setouchi City Historic Site under the name "Tenjinyama Kofun." == Overview == This Kofun is located on the ridge of a hill at the eastern edge of Ushimado Bay, southeast Okayama Prefecture.[http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/kurashi/kankorekishi/rekishi/jyuyobunkazai/shisekikoukou/shijyuyo/1418099490778.html Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun] (Setouchi City website). Ushimado Bay contains five keyhole-shaped Kofuns: this mound, {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun is the oldest among them.On-site explanation board. The Ushimado Tenjin Shrine is located on its southern side. No typeal excavations have been conducted. The mound has a primitive keyhole type, with its front section undeveloped and flat, oriented south toward the sea. The rear section is believed to have two tiers, while the front section has one. Surface features include stone cover (rounded cobblestones) and haniwa fragments (cylindrical and jar-shaped). The burial facility is estimated to be a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, with slab-like stones visible at the rear section's summit. burial goods have not been clearly identified. The construction is estimated to date to the mid-to-late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, during the early Kofun period. Its strategic placement overlooking the sea passage between Ushimado Bay and Maeshima, known as "Karakoto Seto" or "Ushimado Seto," emphasizes the region's political importance during its construction. The site is protected as a Setouchi City Historic Site. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 85 meters * Rear section: ''' Diameter: Approximately 50 meters ''' Height: Approximately 7-8 meters * Front section: ''' Length: Approximately 35 meters ''' Width: Approximately 25-30 meters ''' Height: Approximately 4 meters The 80-meter-class size identifies it as belonging to the highest-Ranking leaders of the Yoshii River's eastern coastal region. The stone slabs presumed to be part of the burial chamber were sourced from {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Kagawa Prefecture. The sea between the mound and Maeshima is known as Karakoto Seto or Ushimado Seto.}]]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300| height =250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1=Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1=bottom | map_dot_label-color1=black | mark1=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.svg | mark-size1=25 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2=Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2=top | map_dot_label-color2=black | mark2=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.svg | mark-size2=25 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3=Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3=bottom | map_dot_label-color3=black | mark3=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.svg | mark-size3=25 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4=(Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4=bottom | map_dot_label-color4=black | mark4=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.svg | mark-size4=25 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5=Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5=right | map_dot_label-color5=black | mark5=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.svg | mark-size5=25 }} '''Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun''' (牛窓天神山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is Historic Site1 as a Setouchi City Historic Site under the name "Tenjinyama Kofun." == Overview == This Kofun is located on the ridge of a hill at the eastern edge of Ushimado Bay, southeast Okayama Prefecture.[http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/kurashi/kankorekishi/rekishi/jyuyobunkazai/shisekikoukou/shijyuyo/1418099490778.html Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun] (Setouchi City website). Ushimado Bay contains five keyhole-shaped Kofuns: this mound, {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun is the oldest among them.On-site explanation board. The Ushimado Tenjin Shrine is located on its southern side. No typeal excavations have been conducted. The mound has a primitive keyhole type, with its front section undeveloped and flat, oriented south toward the sea. The rear section is believed to have two tiers, while the front section has one. Surface features include stone cover (rounded cobblestones) and haniwa fragments (cylindrical and jar-shaped). The burial facility is estimated to be a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, with slab-like stones visible at the rear section's summit. burial goods have not been clearly identified. The construction is estimated to date to the mid-to-late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, during the early Kofun period. Its strategic placement overlooking the sea passage between Ushimado Bay and Maeshima, known as "Karakoto Seto" or "Ushimado Seto," emphasizes the region's political importance during its construction. The site is protected as a Setouchi City Historic Site. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 85 meters * Rear section: ''' Diameter: Approximately 50 meters ''' Height: Approximately 7-8 meters * Front section: ''' Length: Approximately 35 meters ''' Width: Approximately 25-30 meters ''' Height: Approximately 4 meters The 80-meter-class size identifies it as belonging to the highest-Ranking leaders of the Yoshii River's eastern coastal region. The stone slabs presumed to be part of the burial chamber were sourced from {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Kagawa Prefecture. The stone slabs presumed to be part of the burial chamber were sourced from {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Kagawa Prefecture. File:Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun, funcho.jpg|Summit of the rear section File:Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View of the rear section from the front File:Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View of the front section from the rear File:Ushimado Tenjinja, haiden.jpg|Ushimado Tenjin Shrine, located on the southern side == Historic Site == === Setouchi City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' Tenjinyama Kofun{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/91/bunkazai_itiran(20161001).pdf Designated Historic Site List]}} (Setouchi City website). == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not used in this article)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|title=Okayama Prefecture Archaeological Materials Vol. 18|publisher=Okayama Prefecture|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/kurashi/kankorekishi/rekishi/jyuyobunkazai/shisekikoukou/shijyuyo/1418099490778.html Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun] -
