To the left of Kuroshima is {{ill|Maeshima (Okayama)|ja|前島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maeshima (Okayama)|1=Mae-shima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11397116}}, with {{ill|Shōdoshima|en|Shōdoshima|WD=Q2166413|lt=Shodoshima|1=Shōdoshima}} visible in the far background. The sea between the mound and Maeshima is known as Karakoto Seto or Ushimado Seto.}]]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300| height =250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1=Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1=bottom | map_dot_label-color1=black | mark1=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.svg | mark-size1=25 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2=Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2=top | map_dot_label-color2=black | mark2=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.svg | mark-size2=25 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3=Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3=bottom | map_dot_label-color3=black | mark3=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.svg | mark-size3=25 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4=(Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4=bottom | map_dot_label-color4=black | mark4=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.svg | mark-size4=25 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5=Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5=right | map_dot_label-color5=black | mark5=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.svg | mark-size5=25 }} '''Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun''' (牛窓天神山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|1=Ushimado|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570212}}. It is Historic Site1 as a Setouchi City Historic Site under the name "Tenjinyama Kofun." == Overview == This Kofun is located on the ridge of a hill at the eastern edge of Ushimado Bay, southeast Okayama Prefecture.[http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/kurashi/kankorekishi/rekishi/jyuyobunkazai/shisekikoukou/shijyuyo/1418099490778.html Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun] (Setouchi City website). Ushimado Bay contains five keyhole-shaped Kofuns: this mound, {{ill|Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kahoyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}}. Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun is the oldest among them.On-site explanation board. The Ushimado Tenjin Shrine is located on its southern side. No typeal excavations have been conducted. The mound has a primitive keyhole type, with its front section undeveloped and flat, oriented south toward the sea. The rear section is believed to have two tiers, while the front section has one. Surface features include stone cover (rounded cobblestones) and haniwa fragments (cylindrical and jar-shaped). The burial facility is estimated to be a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/vertical stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, with slab-like stones visible at the rear section's summit. burial goods have not been clearly identified. The construction is estimated to date to the mid-to-late {{ill|4th century|en|4th century|WD=Q8099|lt=4th century|1=4th century}}, during the early Kofun period. Its strategic placement overlooking the sea passage between Ushimado Bay and Maeshima, known as "Karakoto Seto" or "Ushimado Seto," emphasizes the region's political importance during its construction. The site is protected as a Setouchi City Historic Site. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 85 meters * Rear section: ''' Diameter: Approximately 50 meters ''' Height: Approximately 7-8 meters * Front section: ''' Length: Approximately 35 meters ''' Width: Approximately 25-30 meters ''' Height: Approximately 4 meters The 80-meter-class size identifies it as belonging to the highest-Ranking leaders of the Yoshii River's eastern coastal region. The stone slabs presumed to be part of the burial chamber were sourced from {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashima|1=Yashima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11465681}} in Kagawa Prefecture. File:Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun, funcho.jpg|Summit of the rear section File:Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View of the rear section from the front File:Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View of the front section from the rear File:Ushimado Tenjinsha, haiden.jpg|Ushimado Tenjin Shrine, located on the southern side == Historic Site == === Setouchi City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' Tenjinyama Kofun{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/91/bunkazai_itiran(20161001).pdf Designated Historic Site List]}} (Setouchi City website). == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not used in this article)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|title=Okayama Prefecture Archaeological Materials Vol. 18|publisher=Okayama Prefecture|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/kurashi/kankorekishi/rekishi/jyuyobunkazai/shisekikoukou/shijyuyo/1418099490778.html Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun] - Setouchi City Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|version=96036079|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:牛窓天神山古墳]] {{Commons category|Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q55523152}} [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]]

The construction is estimated to date to the mid-to-late {{ill|4th century|en|4th century|WD=Q8099|lt=4th century|1=4th century}}, during the early Kofun period. The mound has a primitive keyhole type, with its front section undeveloped and flat, oriented south toward the sea. The rear section is believed to have two tiers, while the front section has one. Surface features include stone cover (rounded cobblestones) and haniwa fragments (cylindrical and jar-shaped). The burial facility is estimated to be a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/vertical stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, with slab-like stones visible at the rear section's summit. burial goods have not been clearly identified. The construction is estimated to date to the mid-to-late {{ill|4th century|en|4th century|WD=Q8099|lt=4th century|1=4th century}}, during the early Kofun period. Its strategic placement overlooking the sea passage between Ushimado Bay and Maeshima, known as "Karakoto Seto" or "Ushimado Seto," emphasizes the region's political importance during its construction. The site is protected as a Setouchi City Historic Site. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 85 meters * Rear section: ''' Diameter: Approximately 50 meters ''' Height: Approximately 7-8 meters * Front section: ''' Length: Approximately 35 meters ''' Width: Approximately 25-30 meters ''' Height: Approximately 4 meters The 80-meter-class size identifies it as belonging to the highest-Ranking leaders of the Yoshii River's eastern coastal region. The stone slabs presumed to be part of the burial chamber were sourced from {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashima|1=Yashima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11465681}} in Kagawa Prefecture. File:Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun, funcho.jpg|Summit of the rear section File:Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View of the rear section from the front File:Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View of the front section from the rear File:Ushimado Tenjinsha, haiden.jpg|Ushimado Tenjin Shrine, located on the southern side == Historic Site == === Setouchi City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' Tenjinyama Kofun{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/91/bunkazai_itiran(20161001).pdf Designated Historic Site List]}} (Setouchi City website). == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not used in this article)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|title=Okayama Prefecture Archaeological Materials Vol. 18|publisher=Okayama Prefecture|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/kurashi/kankorekishi/rekishi/jyuyobunkazai/shisekikoukou/shijyuyo/1418099490778.html Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun] - Setouchi City Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|version=96036079|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:牛窓天神山古墳]] {{Commons category|Ushimado Tenjin-yama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q55523152}} [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

The sea between the mound and Maeshima is known as Karakoto Seto or Ushimado Seto.}]]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300| height =250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1=Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1=bottom | map_dot_label-color1=black | mark1=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.svg | mark-size1=25 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2=Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2=top | map_dot_label-color2=black | mark2=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.svg | mark-size2=25 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3=Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3=bottom | map_dot_label-color3=black | mark3=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.svg | mark-size3=25 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4=(Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4=bottom | map_dot_label-color4=black | mark4=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.svg | mark-size4=25 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5=Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5=right | map_dot_label-color5=black | mark5=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.svg | mark-size5=25 }} '''Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun''' (牛窓天神山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpōkōenfun|en|Zenpōkōenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpōkōenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|1=Ushimado|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570212}}. It is Historic Site1 as a Setouchi City Historic Site under the name "Tenjinyama Kofun." == Overview == This Kofun is located on the ridge of a hill at the eastern edge of Ushimado Bay, southeast Okayama Prefecture.[http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/kurashi/kankorekishi/rekishi/jyuyobunkazai/shisekikoukou/shijyuyo/1418099490778.html Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun] (Setouchi City website). Ushimado Bay contains five keyhole-shaped Kofuns: this mound, {{ill|Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kahoyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}}. Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun is the oldest among them.On-site explanation board. The Ushimado Tenjin Shrine is located on its southern side. No typeal excavations have been conducted. The mound has a primitive keyhole type, with its front section undeveloped and flat, oriented south toward the sea. The rear section is believed to have two tiers, while the front section has one. Surface features include stone cover (rounded cobblestones) and haniwa fragments (cylindrical and jar-shaped). The burial facility is estimated to be a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/vertical stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, with slab-like stones visible at the rear section's summit. burial goods have not been clearly identified. The construction is estimated to date to the mid-to-late {{ill|4th century|en|4th century|WD=Q8099|lt=4th century|1=4th century}}, during the early Kofun period. Its strategic placement overlooking the sea passage between Ushimado Bay and Maeshima, known as "Karakoto Seto" or "Ushimado Seto," emphasizes the region's political importance during its construction. The site is protected as a Setouchi City Historic Site. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 85 meters * Rear section: ''' Diameter: Approximately 50 meters ''' Height: Approximately 7-8 meters * Front section: ''' Length: Approximately 35 meters ''' Width: Approximately 25-30 meters ''' Height: Approximately 4 meters The 80-meter-class size identifies it as belonging to the highest-Ranking leaders of the Yoshii River's eastern coastal region. The stone slabs presumed to be part of the burial chamber were sourced from {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashima|1=Yashima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11465681}} in Kagawa Prefecture. The sea between the mound and Maeshima is known as Karakoto Seto or Ushimado Seto.}]]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300| height =250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1=Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1=bottom | map_dot_label-color1=black | mark1=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.svg | mark-size1=25 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2=Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2=top | map_dot_label-color2=black | mark2=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.svg | mark-size2=25 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3=Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3=bottom | map_dot_label-color3=black | mark3=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.svg | mark-size3=25 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4=(Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4=bottom | map_dot_label-color4=black | mark4=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.svg | mark-size4=25 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5=Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5=right | map_dot_label-color5=black | mark5=Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.svg | mark-size5=25 }} '''Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun''' (牛窓天神山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|Zenpō-kōen-fun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun}} located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|1=Ushimado|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570212}}. It is Historic Site1 as a Setouchi City Historic Site under the name "Tenjinyama Kofun." == Overview == This Kofun is located on the ridge of a hill at the eastern edge of Ushimado Bay, southeast Okayama Prefecture.[http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/kurashi/kankorekishi/rekishi/jyuyobunkazai/shisekikoukou/shijyuyo/1418099490778.html Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun] (Setouchi City website). Ushimado Bay contains five keyhole-shaped Kofuns: this mound, {{ill|Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kahoyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}}. Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun is the oldest among them.On-site explanation board. The Ushimado Tenjin Shrine is located on its southern side. No typeal excavations have been conducted. The mound has a primitive keyhole type, with its front section undeveloped and flat, oriented south toward the sea. The rear section is believed to have two tiers, while the front section has one. Surface features include stone cover (rounded cobblestones) and haniwa fragments (cylindrical and jar-shaped). The burial facility is estimated to be a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/vertical stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, with slab-like stones visible at the rear section's summit. burial goods have not been clearly identified. The construction is estimated to date to the mid-to-late {{ill|4th century|en|4th century|WD=Q8099|lt=4th century|1=4th century}}, during the early Kofun period. Its strategic placement overlooking the sea passage between Ushimado Bay and Maeshima, known as "Karakoto Seto" or "Ushimado Seto," emphasizes the region's political importance during its construction. The site is protected as a Setouchi City Historic Site. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 85 meters * Rear section: ''' Diameter: Approximately 50 meters ''' Height: Approximately 7-8 meters * Front section: ''' Length: Approximately 35 meters ''' Width: Approximately 25-30 meters ''' Height: Approximately 4 meters The 80-meter-class size identifies it as belonging to the highest-Ranking leaders of the Yoshii River's eastern coastal region. The stone slabs presumed to be part of the burial chamber were sourced from {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashima|1=Yashima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11465681}} in Kagawa Prefecture.
